decision.decision: 08a BUILDS, unconditionally, the legacy worklist FILTER as the exact-process baseline: pharmacy worklist endpoints surface only prescriptions whose linked PatientBill.status is PAID|COVERED for OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER, and PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED for INPATIENT (VERIFIED = inpatient credit, NOT insurer-verification — D18). The clinical dispense terminal (issue_medicine -> NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN, issued==qty all-or-nothing, hard negative-stock guard, FEFO + PrescriptionBatch lot-trace, OUT card) carries NO terminal bill-status check in the baseline — matching legacy PatientResource.java:3199-3293 exactly. The "hard dispense gate" is a DEVIATION and is NOT in the exact-process baseline. IF the engagement-lead elects the hardening, it MUST be implemented as the already-ratified CR-05 SettlementPolicy pattern at the clinical edge: call SettlementPolicy.requireSettled(...) against the prescription's LOCAL settled flag (V36, flipped by BillSettledEvent) — NOT via BillingQueries.getBillStatus. Option (b) AS LITERALLY PHRASED in Q1 (gate via BillingQueries.getBillStatus) MUST NOT be built: ADR-0008 §6 Addendum scopes that read seam to the add_report bill-gate ONLY and explicitly forbids worklist/settlement gates from using it.
decision.rationale: Three things resolve this. (1) No ADR pre-decides a dispense pay-gate: ADR-0017 governs only stock-decrement concurrency (no payment check); the ADR-0008 §6 Addendum actively NARROWS the getBillStatus seam to add_report only and states all settlement gates and worklist filters MUST keep using the local settled flag — so the ADR text points away from Q1's option (b). (2) The charter default is reproduce-legacy + CR-the-improvement: legacy enforces payment ONLY as a worklist FILTER (PatientResource.java:4347/4364/4381/4410), with the issue_medicine terminal having no pay check and being bypassable by direct call (verified by reading 3199-3293) — so filter-only IS the baseline and the hard gate is a deliberate security/integrity deviation. (3) The identical question was already adjudicated for billing as CR-04-05 / SettlementPolicy: classified as net-new safety hardening that CHANGES legacy behaviour, requiring engagement-lead sign-off with HDE input on emergency-care safety. The ratified SettlementPolicy already implements the safe form (local-flag evaluation at the clinical edge; insurance auto-pass, inpatient pass, emergency bypass) so it cannot block insured/inpatient-credit/emergency dispensing. Clinical impact: filter-only (baseline) reproduces a known integrity gap — a direct API call can dispense an unpaid CASH outpatient's medicine; clinically this is a financial-leakage / theft-of-service risk, not a patient-safety hazard (no clinical harm to the patient from dispensing prescribed medicine). The hard gate via SettlementPolicy closes that leak but introduces a patient-safety hazard IF applied bluntly (blocking emergency/inpatient/insured dispense) — which is exactly why SettlementPolicy's carve-outs are mandatory and why a naive getBillStatus gate is the WRONG mechanism. Because the deviation decision and emergency-safety acceptance were explicitly reserved to the engagement-lead under CR-05, I do not decide it here — I fix the baseline and define the only safe hardening path.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0008 §6 Addendum (2026-06-04, inc-06A C4): getBillStatus read seam scoped to add_report bill-gate ONLY; worklist/settlement gates MUST use the local settled flag — this DECIDES that Q1 option (b) "via BillingQueries.getBillStatus" is not permitted. ADR-0017 is silent on a dispense pay-gate (governs stock-decrement locking only). The governing prior decision is CR-04-05 / CR-05 (SettlementPolicy, RATIFIED scoped; billing increment 04 §EPIC 5), not an ADR.
decision.crNeeded: No NEW CR needed — covered by existing ratified CR-05 (pay-before-service / SettlementPolicy). The dispense terminal is simply an additional clinical-edge enforcement point under CR-05's existing scope. Engagement-lead need only confirm CR-05 enforcement EXTENDS to the pharmacy dispense terminal (the only open item is the owner release gate, not a fresh CR). If the owner instead wants enforcement via a billing read-back rather than the local flag, THAT would need a new CR to amend ADR-0008 §6 — and is NOT recommended.
decision.buildImpact: 08a MUST build: (1) the pharmacy worklist as a FILTER (PAID|COVERED for OUT/OUTSIDER; +VERIFIED for INPATIENT) — unconditional, exact-process; (2) the dispense terminal with NO terminal bill-status check in the baseline. 08a MUST NOT build: any hard dispense gate that calls BillingQueries.getBillStatus (forbidden by ADR-0008 §6 Addendum); any blanket/unconditional pay-block that ignores the insurance/inpatient/emergency carve-outs. CONDITIONAL on engagement-lead sign-off ONLY: a hard dispense gate implemented as SettlementPolicy.requireSettled(settled, paymentType, inpatient, emergency, billUid) read against Prescription.settled (V36 local flag, BillSettledEvent-driven) at the issue_medicine seam, throwing 422 PAY_BEFORE_SERVICE. Note the as-built PrescriptionRepository (lines 133-138) deliberately leaves the pharmacy queue un-settled-filtered with a code comment that pharmacy queues differ; 08a's filter design must reconcile that comment with the legacy filter behaviour (the legacy worklist DID filter on bill status — the as-built omission is itself a drift to flag to the BA). QA/parity: the golden-master harness must assert the FILTER behaviour and MUST NOT assert a terminal rejection on direct dispense unless/until the gate is approved (mirrors the N7/N13 "filter not gate" lesson). Add ErrorCode reuse: PAY_BEFORE_SERVICE (already exists from CR-05) — do not mint a new PRESCRIPTION_UNPAID code.
decision.ownerActionRequired: Engagement-lead must decide whether CR-05's pay-before-service hard gate EXTENDS to the pharmacy dispense terminal (deviation from legacy filter-only behaviour), with HDE confirming no emergency/inpatient/insured dispensing regression — exactly the sign-off CR-04-05 reserved to the engagement-lead with HDE input. Until that sign-off, 08a ships filter-only (legacy-faithful) and the hard gate is NOT built. HDE position for that decision: the SettlementPolicy local-flag gate WITH its existing insurance/inpatient/emergency carve-outs is clinically safe and recommended (it closes a real theft-of-service / financial-leakage gap without a patient-safety hazard); a getBillStatus-based or blanket gate is NOT safe and not permitted.

challenge.reason:
Verified against source on all three adversarial axes; the decision is charter-compliant and builds nothing beyond verified legacy behaviour without a CR.

AXIS 1 (ADR contradiction): NONE. I read ADR-0008 §6 Addendum directly (docs/adr/0008-bounded-context-decomposition.md:74-93): getBillStatus is scoped to the add_report bill-gate ONLY, and it states verbatim "All settlement gates (open_consultation, the lab/radiology/procedure worklist filters) MUST continue to use the local settled flag — they are NOT permitted to use this read seam." This DECIDES that Q1 option (b) ('gate via BillingQueries.getBillStatus') is forbidden — exactly as the decision claims. The same is reaffirmed in BillingQueries.java:12-21 javadoc. ADR-0017 is indeed silent on a dispense pay-gate (it governs only stock-decrement concurrency). I did NOT trust the ADR blindly: the Addendum's claim that the local settled flag is 'provably insufficient' is add_report-specific (legacy reads LIVE PatientBill.status there, with deferred cash-PAID→settled propagation). For the SETTLEMENT-gate use, the local flag is authoritative — ConsultationSettlementListener.java:104-142 flips it via @TransactionalEventListener(BEFORE_COMMIT) in the SAME billing transaction, and 05 §5 (11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:84-93) sets it true at booking for COVERED/NONE/inpatient. No deferred window for this gate; no contradiction.

AXIS 2 (smuggled deviation): NONE. The baseline builds only verified legacy behaviour. I confirmed the worklist FILTER statuses verbatim in legacy PatientResource.java:4347 (OUTPATIENT → PAID|COVERED), :4364 (OUTSIDER → PAID|COVERED), :4381 (INPATIENT → PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED), :4410 (per-patient list → PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED) — matching the decision's filter spec and the D18 inpatient-credit reading of VERIFIED. I confirmed the dispense terminal (PatientResource.java:3199-3293) has NO bill-status check — guards are exactly: exists, status==NOT-GIVEN, valid issued, issued==qty, then GIVEN + negative-stock guard + stock-card OUT + deductBatch. The hard gate is correctly held OUT of the baseline and made CONDITIONAL on engagement-lead sign-off — consistent with ratified CR-INC05-01 ('the direct-API-bypass gap is reproduced, not closed', 05/11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:22). No new CR invented (correctly leans on existing ratified CR-05); no new ErrorCode minted (PAY_BEFORE_SERVICE already exists at ErrorCode.java:98; PayBeforeServiceException exists; PRESCRIPTION_UNPAID does not). The mandated hardening mechanism — SettlementPolicy.requireSettled against Prescription.settled (V36) — matches the ratified seam (SettlementPolicy.java:60-65 with insurance/inpatient/emergency carve-outs intact).

AXIS 3 (classification & owner action): CORRECT. OWNER_RELEASE_GATE is apt. CR-05 as ratified (04/11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:11) scopes clinical-side wiring to 'inc-05/06' and frames enforcement at accept(); the pharmacy dispense terminal is an inc-08 enforcement point NOT enumerated in CR-05, so confirming the extension is genuinely an engagement-lead+HDE decision, correctly NOT decided by the specialist. Ships filter-only until sign-off. Correctly flags the as-built PrescriptionRepository.java:132-143 comment ('settled filter is intentionally omitted... pharmacy queues are different') as a drift to reconcile with the legacy filter behaviour for the BA.

decision.decision: REPRODUCE CLIENT-TRUST (exact-process). 08a/08b must treat the operating pharmacy as a client-supplied parameter on every dispensing/stock/transfer call (legacy pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id -> modern pharmacyUid; pure uid rename, behaviour preserved) and must NOT verify the authenticated user against any pharmacist->pharmacy affiliation. There is NO pharmacy_staff table, NO pharmacist->pharmacy / user->pharmacy field, and NO write-time affiliation check in legacy (Pharmacist.java:44-72 = OneToOne User only; Pharmacy.java:34-54 / User.java have no link; no Collection<Pharmacy> anywhere; savePharmacySaleOrder does no affiliation check). The planning doc's pharmacy_staff M:N table enforced as a 403 gate and 'seeded in I01' is PHANTOM (D13) and must NOT be built, seeded, or referenced as a cross-increment dependency. The only staff->location precedent (StorePerson @ManyToMany Collection<Store>) is a SOFT UI filter for stores only, never a write gate, and must not be imported onto the pharmacy path. Endpoint authorization remains role/privilege-based per @PreAuthorize (resolved under Q5); the operating-pharmacy value is NOT an authorization input in the exact-process baseline. Introducing the pharmacy_staff affiliation 403 gate is a DEVIATION that requires an approved CR before any schema/seed/check is built; until that CR is approved no such artifact exists. The 'should pharmacy become stricter than stores' policy question and the increment-ownership-of-schema/seed question are owner decisions deferred to the CR, not decided here. NOTE: this is distinct from N18 (legacy default-open posture: no anyRequest().authenticated(), anonymous fall-through, HMAC secret 'secret', commented token recheck) -- closing that deny-by-default is the legitimate APPROVED_HARDENING item and does NOT require or imply a per-staff pharmacy affiliation model.
decision.rationale: Legacy has NO pharmacist->pharmacy affiliation and NO server-side scoping; the operating pharmacy is client-supplied and trusted per call (PHARM-1 confirmed: Pharmacist.java:44-72, Pharmacy.java:34-54, User.java, plus negative grep for Collection<Pharmacy> and findByPharmacist*). Under the charter the business PROCESS must stay identical, so the baseline is reproduce-legacy. The pharmacy_staff M:N 403 gate has zero legacy basis (D13 PHANTOM) and contradicts observed behaviour, so it cannot enter the exact-process baseline silently -- it is a deliberate net-new access boundary = DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR. It does NOT qualify as APPROVED_HARDENING: client-trust on the operating-pharmacy param is a deliberate legacy design choice, not an unauthenticated/injection/PHI-leak vulnerability that the charter would refuse to call 'process'; per security guardrails, legacy access boundaries are source-of-truth and privileges must not be invented/promoted without an approved CR. The real vulnerability in this area is the SEPARATE default-open posture (N18), which is the correct hardening vehicle and needs no affiliation model. Whether pharmacy should become stricter than stores, and which increment owns the schema/seed if approved, are scope decisions reserved to the engagement owner via the CR -- not for the architect to pre-decide.
decision.adrCitation: none -- ADRs silent (ADR-0017 covers concurrency/locking; ADR-0009 covers numbering/sequences; neither addresses pharmacy session scoping or staff->location affiliation). Governed by the engagement charter exact-process principle and by PHARM-1 (factual premise confirmed) + D13 (PHANTOM remedy).
decision.crNeeded: CR-Q2-PHARMACY-STAFF-AFFILIATION: Introduce net-new pharmacist->pharmacy (pharmacy_staff M:N) affiliation enforced as a write-time 403 scoping gate -- DEVIATION from legacy client-trust; owner must approve scope, decide stricter-than-stores policy, and assign the increment owning the schema/seed (and gate the affiliation-write endpoint with ADMIN-ACCESS) before any build.
decision.buildImpact: 08a/08b BUILD: accept pharmacyUid as a client-supplied call parameter on all dispense/stock/transfer operations (rename of legacy pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id; behaviour preserved); enforce endpoint authorization via role/privilege @PreAuthorize only (per Q5); use pharmacyUid solely to select the stock source, NOT as an authorization subject. 08a/08b MUST NOT BUILD: any pharmacy_staff (or User->Pharmacy / Pharmacist->Pharmacy) table, column, migration (V39+), seed, JWT pharmacy claim, or write-time 403 affiliation check; MUST NOT add an I01 'pharmacy_staff seeded' dependency; MUST NOT copy StorePerson->Store onto pharmacy. Keep this item on the 'MUST be RE-SCOPED out of exact-process' list. SEPARATE/UNAFFECTED: closing the N18 default-open posture (deny-by-default) proceeds as approved hardening independent of this decision.
decision.ownerActionRequired: Engagement-lead (with BA, and security-architect to own the privilege-to-endpoint mapping if approved) must decide whether to raise/approve CR-Q2: (1) approve the net-new pharmacy_staff affiliation 403 gate as a deliberate deviation, (2) rule on whether pharmacy may be stricter than stores (which use only a soft filter) or whether parity should be kept, and (3) assign which increment owns the pharmacy_staff schema/seed and update I01 traceability. Until the owner approves the CR, the exact-process baseline (client-trust) stands and 08a/08b build it as-is. The N18 deny-by-default hardening is a separate already-flagged item and is not blocked by this gate.

challenge.reason:
SUBSTANCE IS LARGELY SOUND, BUT THE ADR CITATION IS WRONG -- the decision declares "ADRs silent" when an on-point ADR exists, so it fails the citation axis (per the default-to-REVISE rule for ADR mis-citation).

AXIS 1 -- Contradicts/mis-cites a ratified ADR? YES (mis-cite). The factual premise PHARM-1 is fully verified against the legacy oracle: Pharmacist.java:62-65 = @OneToOne User only, no Collection<Pharmacy>; Pharmacy.java:34-54 has no user/pharmacist link; grep for Collection<Pharmacy> and findByPharmacist* = zero hits; savePharmacySaleOrder (PatientServiceImpl.java:3334-3339) and issue_medicine (PatientResource.java:3199-3239) take the pharmacy from a client-supplied value and never check it against the authenticated user (user is used only for createdBy/approvedBy stamping). StorePerson.stores (StorePerson.java:63-65) consumed only by the read-only getStoresByStorePerson (StoreServiceImpl.java:147-164) = soft UI filter, never a write gate. So "no pharmacist->pharmacy affiliation, no write-time 403 gate" is correct. HOWEVER the decision's adrCitation "none -- ADRs silent... neither addresses pharmacy session scoping or staff->location affiliation" is FALSE: ADR-0020 (Facility Scoping & Tenancy) is squarely on point. ADR-0020 §Decision.4 explicitly rules OUT per-unit affiliation for pharmacists (only ClinicClinician + StoreStaff exist) -- which AGREES with refusing pharmacy_staff -- but §Decision.5 / §Consequences / §Implementation-notes name PHARM-1 by ID and mandate a request-scoped "selected working pharmacy" pharmacyUid (header/session) that the server must "reject stock ops without." An adversarial review cannot let "ADRs silent" stand on a question an ADR titles and addresses by name (note ADR-0020 is status "Proposed," not yet ratified, so it is not binding -- but it is not silent either).

AXIS 2 -- Smuggles a deviation into the baseline without a CR? NO for the affiliation gate (the decision correctly refuses it), but the FRAMING is imprecise in a way that risks the opposite error. The decision correctly identifies the pharmacy_staff M:N 403 affiliation gate as PHANTOM (matches D13 / reconciliation line 161 / open-question Q2) and correctly routes it to CR-Q2 -- this is charter-compliant and does NOT smuggle a deviation. The planning doc that DID smuggle it (08-pharmacy-inventory-procurement.md:134, 141, 169: "validates the authenticated user is affiliated... pharmacy_staff seeded in I01... 403 Forbidden") is correctly neutralized. BUT the decision's headline "REPRODUCE CLIENT-TRUST (exact-process)... treat the operating pharmacy as a client-supplied parameter... behaviour preserved" conflates two separable things: (a) the affiliation check user-in-pharmacy = no legacy basis = correctly CR; and (b) whether pharmacyUid must be a present, server-validated working-pharmacy context. Legacy ALREADY requires-and-validates the pharmacy: issue_medicine takes a mandatory pharmacy_id @RequestParam and throws NotFoundException("Pharmacy not found") if it does not resolve (PatientResource.java:3202,3207-3210), and savePharmacySaleOrder dereferences order.getPharmacy().getName() unconditionally. So "require a present/resolvable pharmacyUid and reject the op if absent" is FAITHFUL to legacy and consistent with ADR-0020's "reject stock ops without it" -- it is NOT the affiliation deviation. Labeling pharmacyUid as merely "client-supplied/trusted, behaviour preserved" risks (i) building it as an optional, unvalidated body field (a regression vs legacy's NotFoundException-on-missing-pharmacy), and (ii) silently taking a position contrary to live ADR-0020 without flagging the conflict to the owner.

AXIS 3 -- Classification correct / owner action correctly flagged? MOSTLY YES. EXACT_PROCESS_REPRODUCE for the operating-pharmacy parameter and DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR for the pharmacy_staff affiliation gate is the right split, and correctly NOT APPROVED_HARDENING (client-trust on the pharmacy param is a legacy design choice, not an injection/PHI/anon-access vuln). The N18 default-open separation is correct and verified (PharmacyResource.java:298 has @PreAuthorize commented out; this is the real hardening vehicle and needs no affiliation model). The deferral of the stricter-than-stores policy and the schema/seed-ownership question to the owner via CR-Q2 is correctly NOT pre-decided. The one owner-facing gap: the decision does not surface that ADR-0020 (a) already rules out pharmacist affiliation -- corroborating the no-pharmacy_staff position -- and (b) already mandates a server-required working-pharmacy pharmacyUid, which the build must honor and which is distinct from (not a back-door to) the affiliation gate.
challenge.correction: Keep the substantive build instruction (08a/08b: pharmacyUid is a per-call parameter used ONLY to select the stock source; endpoint authz via @PreAuthorize per Q5; MUST NOT build any pharmacy_staff / User->Pharmacy / Pharmacist->Pharmacy table, column, V39+ migration, seed, JWT pharmacy claim, or write-time 403 affiliation check; MUST NOT add an I01 "pharmacy_staff seeded" dependency; MUST NOT copy StorePerson->Store onto pharmacy; affiliation gate stays a DEVIATION behind CR-Q2). Apply three corrections: (1) Fix the ADR citation from "none -- ADRs silent" to "ADR-0020 (Facility Scoping & Tenancy, status Proposed -- not yet ratified) is ON POINT, not silent: it names PHARM-1 and §Decision.4 independently confirms NO pharmacist->pharmacy affiliation (corroborating this decision), while §Decision.5/§Consequences/§Implementation-notes require a server-enforced 'selected working pharmacy' pharmacyUid context. ADR-0017 and ADR-0009 remain off-topic for scoping." (2) Re-state the baseline precisely: do NOT characterize it as 'reproduce client-trust, behaviour preserved' (which reads as an optional/unvalidated field and as overriding ADR-0020). State instead: pharmacyUid is REQUIRED and server-VALIDATED to resolve (reject a missing/unresolvable pharmacy -- legacy already does this via NotFoundException at PatientResource.java:3207-3210 and the unconditional deref at PatientServiceImpl.java:3334), but is NOT validated against the authenticated user (no affiliation check) -- that user-in-pharmacy check is the only DEVIATION and stays behind CR-Q2. Requiring presence/resolution is exact-process-faithful AND consistent with ADR-0020; it is not the affiliation gate. (3) Add an explicit owner note: because ADR-0020 (Proposed) already mandates a server-required working-pharmacy pharmacyUid AND already excludes pharmacist affiliation, the engagement-lead should either ratify ADR-0020 as-is (build presence-required pharmacyUid, no affiliation) or, if a stricter affiliation gate is wanted, approve CR-Q2 -- but the architect must NOT silently resolve pharmacyUid as merely 'trusted' in a way that diverges from the on-point ADR. CR-Q2 scope, the stricter-than-stores policy, the schema/seed increment owner, and the I01 traceability fix remain owner decisions, unchanged.

decision.decision: DROP three-way match from the inc-08 exact-process baseline entirely. 08b builds ONLY the real legacy reconciliation: (1) two-way quantity check receivedQty <= orderedQty, and (2) the per-line batch-sum integrity rule sum(GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch.qty) == detail.receivedQty, which together gate the per-LINE NOT-VERIFIED -> VERIFIED transition; GRN HEADER remains PENDING -> APPROVED only. NO SupplierInvoice entity, NO GRN-detail invoice-qty field, NO payment-release gate, NO /three-way-match/validate endpoint are built. The desired new feature (real three-way match: LPO=GRN=SupplierInvoice qty with a payment-release workflow) is PARKED as a candidate NEW-FEATURE CR for engagement-lead + finance/HDE; I do not decide whether to build it -- that is an owner scope decision. Until/unless that CR is raised and approved, three-way match does not exist in the backlog.
decision.rationale: Three-way match is a confirmed PHANTOM (D11): zero legacy basis -- no SupplierInvoice entity, no invoice-qty field, no payment-release gate anywhere in the legacy oracle (reconciliation doc line 86; raw cites lines 1129-1133, 1512, 1561). The legacy procurement reconciliation is strictly two-way (receivedQty <= orderedQty) plus the per-line batch-sum integrity rule (sum(batch.qty)==receivedQty), gating the per-line NOT-VERIFIED->VERIFIED transition (doc section H line 62; raw line 1139). The charter default for a tempting modern improvement that has no legacy basis is reproduce-legacy in the baseline and treat the improvement as a deviation requiring an approved CR before any code. This is NOT a security vulnerability the charter would re-label 'process', so it is not APPROVED_HARDENING; it is net-new financial/control scope. It is the exact inc-06 trap inverted: the planning doc drifted toward an idealized modern procurement-control design over a greenfield stub with no as-built to constrain it.
decision.adrCitation: none -- ADRs silent on three-way match. No ratified ADR decides it: ADR-0009 section 'Prefix table (complete, supersedes all other ADRs)' (lines 122-137) lists only GRN and LPO for procurement -- NO SupplierInvoice document type. ADR-0008 line 60, ADR-0007 line 16, ADR-0013 line 110 reference procurement solely as the existing two-document 'GRN approve -> stock + stock-card + batch + purchase + LPO status all in one tx'. ADR-0015 line 10/16 mentions 'supplier invoices' only as a generic future file-attachment target, NOT a ratified procurement reconciliation workflow. Therefore the question is open, not pre-decided.
decision.crNeeded: CR: NEW FEATURE -- Three-way procurement match (net-new SupplierInvoice entity + GRN-detail invoice-qty capture + payment-release gate + /three-way-match/validate endpoint); safety-critical financial; no legacy basis -- requires engagement-lead + finance/HDE approval before any backlog entry or code.
decision.buildImpact: 08b MUST build: GoodsReceivedNoteDetail per-line state machine NOT-VERIFIED -> VERIFIED with the dual reconciliation gate [receivedQty <= orderedQty] AND [sum(GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch.qty) == detail.receivedQty]; GRN header PENDING -> APPROVED only (no header VERIFIED/REJECTED/RETURNED); approve() hard-gates that EVERY line is VERIFIED before posting stock/card/batch/Purchase-ledger and flipping LPO -> RECEIVED, all in one transaction; one-GRN-per-LPO with store-match + LPO==SUBMITTED preconditions. These get their own acceptance criteria (this is N3, the REAL reconciliation gate). 08b MUST NOT build: any SupplierInvoice entity/table, any invoice-qty field on GRN detail, any payment-release gate or approval-blocking on invoice match, any /three-way-match/validate (or equivalent) endpoint, any DocumentType/sequence for a supplier invoice. QA must NOT write parity tests asserting three-way-match behaviour; the parity oracle for procurement reconciliation is two-way + batch-sum only.
decision.ownerActionRequired: engagement-lead + finance/HDE must decide whether the net-new three-way match feature is wanted at all. If yes, engagement-lead approves the parked NEW-FEATURE CR at the phase gate BEFORE any implementation; finance/HDE must supply the real reconciliation semantics (invoice-qty source, tolerance rules, what 'payment release' gates and who authorizes it) since there is no legacy oracle to extract them from. I (BA) maintain and impact-assess the CR register but do NOT approve it. No owner action is required to proceed with the baseline drop -- the exact-process build of two-way + batch-sum reconciliation proceeds now without waiting on this decision.

challenge.reason: Verified against the legacy oracle and ADRs; the decision is charter-compliant on all three axes. AXIS 1 (ADR contradiction): NONE. ADR-0009's prefix table (docs/adr/0009-money-and-numbering.md lines 122-137, marked "complete, supersedes all other ADRs") lists only GRN+LPO for procurement, no SupplierInvoice doc type/sequence; ADR-0015 (lines 10,16) names "supplier invoices" only as a future file-attachment target; grep of docs/adr for three-way/ThreeWayMatch/SupplierInvoice = zero hits. "ADRs silent, question open" is true and not blindly trusted (the decision correctly leaves the orthogonal ADR-0009 seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no vs as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no drift alone, since it concerns TO sequences not SupplierInvoice). AXIS 2 (smuggled deviation): NONE, either direction. Dropping three-way match is not a deviation — it is a confirmed PHANTOM: the discovery grep for SupplierInvoice|invoiceQty|threeWay|matchQty|invoiceQuantity|ThreeWayMatch across the entire legacy com.orbix.api returned ZERO hits (raw JSON line 1131); build-plan.md does not mandate it; the inc-08 doc's PROCESS.md §16.11 cites a non-existent spec. What 08b is told to build (per-line NOT-VERIFIED->VERIFIED gated by receivedQty<=orderedQty AND sum(GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch.qty)==detail.receivedQty; header PENDING->APPROVED only; approve() hard-gates every line; one-GRN-per-LPO with store-match + LPO==SUBMITTED) is verbatim verified legacy behaviour (reconciliation §H line 62; raw lines 1131-1141, 1512, 1561; N3 line 118) with NO invented header VERIFIED state, payment gate, or SoD codes. AXIS 3 (classification + owner action): correct. DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR is right — a real three-way match is net-new safety-critical financial scope, not security hardening, so not APPROVED_HARDENING. The decision PARKS the new-feature question for engagement-lead + finance/HDE, explicitly disclaims deciding it, requires finance/HDE to supply semantics (no legacy oracle), and correctly proceeds with the pure-reproduction baseline now without blocking on the owner decision. This is the inc-06 phantom-feature trap correctly rejected, with the worklist-filter-vs-hard-gate distinction correctly preserved (header filter not promoted to a gate; the real gate is the per-line one legacy actually enforces).

decision.decision: BUILD PER ADR-0017 §2 (with §1 for the split). Q4 is NOT an open question and needs NO new CR. Stock decrement on every mutating path (clinical dispense, OTC dispense, transfer TO-issue, GRN approve) acquires a PESSIMISTIC_WRITE row lock on the stock-balance row and FEFO batches BEFORE the negative-stock guard runs: repos expose lockBalance(location,item) and lockFefoForDispense(...) (ADR-0017 §2). Decrement service order is fixed: acquire lock -> re-read balance -> assert qty>=requested (preserve the legacy refusal message verbatim) -> decrement balance -> FEFO-decrement batches -> append stock_card row -> commit (ADR-0017 Implementation notes). The @Version-on-transitions / pessimistic-on-decrement SPLIT is confirmed: @Version optimistic lock is the default for all non-stock general updates and status transitions, stale write -> 409 STALE_ENTITY (ADR-0017 §1); pessimistic row lock is reserved for the high-contention decrement path only (§2). Deterministic lock ordering (balance before batches; items in uid order within a multi-line GRN/transfer) + statement/lock timeout -> 409 STOCK_CONTENDED is mandatory to prevent deadlock (ADR-0017 Consequences). ADR-0017 already justifies this as exact-process-preserving: the legacy negative-stock REFUSAL is preserved (same exception surface, now 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK / 409 on contention), only the serialization mechanism changes — a correctness improvement, not a behaviour change (ADR-0017 Consequences + Exact-process impact). ONE carve-out that is NOT decided by Q4: the §2 phrase "batches in expiry-ascending order, null expiry last" embeds the FEFO NULLS-LAST ordering, which changes WHICH lot dispenses vs legacy's silent null-expiry exclusion. That user-visible lot-selection change is Q8 (HDE-owned), not part of the locking decision — 08a builds the LOCK as ratified but must NOT treat NULLS-LAST as settled exact-process until Q8 resolves.
decision.rationale: I read ADR-0017 verbatim. §2 decides pessimistic-write locking for stock decrement (lockBalance + lockFefoForDispense, guard-after-lock); §1 decides @Version optimistic locking as the default for non-stock updates/transitions with 409 STALE_ENTITY. The exact tension Q4 raises — is the lock a legacy-violating deviation or a permitted hardening — is already adjudicated inside the ADR: its Context explicitly identifies legacy stock decrement as a check-then-act race with no lock and no @Version, and its Decision/Consequences/Exact-process sections rule that adding the lock PRESERVES the legacy negative-stock REFUSAL process (same exception, same refusal) while making it race-safe, classified as a correctness improvement and NOT a behaviour change. That is precisely the charter-blessed "modernize the mechanism, preserve the process" pattern. Under the charter, a ratified ADR decision is binding and is not re-litigated, so the reconciliation doc's D6/D7/Q4 "net-new hardening => DEVIATION" framing (written from the pure legacy-extraction lens) is superseded for resolution purposes: the deviation has already been ratified into the ADR, so no fresh CR is required. The @Version/pessimistic split is internally consistent and confirmed acceptable given legacy's structural return-existing-doc idempotency on TO/RN create (those are transitions, covered by @Version, not the decrement hot path). I did NOT fold Q8 (FEFO null-expiry => NULLS LAST) into this answer — that is a separate user-visible lot-selection change owned by HDE, even though §2's wording touches it.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0017 §2 (Pessimistic row lock for stock decrement: @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) on stock_balance + stock_batch, lockBalance/lockFefoForDispense, negative-stock guard AFTER the lock); ADR-0017 §1 (Optimistic @Version by default, stale write -> 409 STALE_ENTITY); ADR-0017 Consequences + "Exact-process impact" (legacy negative-stock REFUSAL preserved; only the serialization mechanism changes — correctness improvement, not behaviour change); ADR-0017 Implementation notes (decrement service order; STALE_ENTITY/STOCK_CONTENDED ErrorCodes; locking is intra-module, no new cross-module edges). Note: the ADR file header reads "Proposed", but the binding engagement charter and project memory record ADR-0017 §2 as RATIFIED for inc-08, so it is treated as a decided, non-re-litigable ADR here.
decision.crNeeded: none (Q4 is ADR_PRE_DECIDED; no new CR). The NULLS-LAST lot-selection sub-point rides on Q8 (HDE), which is a separate open question, not a Q4 CR.
decision.buildImpact: 08a (dispensing + stock core) MUST: (1) put @Version on the shared base entity so all prescription/OTC-header/transfer/LPO/GRN transitions get optimistic locking with 409 STALE_ENTITY; (2) implement lockBalance(pharmacyUid,itemUid) and lockFefoForDispense(...) repo methods with @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) + a javax.persistence.lock.timeout hint and a PG statement_timeout; (3) order the clinical-dispense decrement exactly as ADR-0017 Implementation notes (lock -> re-read -> assert qty>=requested with the legacy refusal message -> decrement balance -> FEFO-decrement batches -> append stock_card OUT row -> commit); (4) add ErrorCodes INSUFFICIENT_STOCK (422) and STOCK_CONTENDED (409) plus STALE_ENTITY (409); (5) include the two Testcontainers concurrency parity cases (two parallel dispenses of the last unit -> exactly one succeeds, one refused; two parallel requestNo calls -> no duplicate). 08b MUST apply the SAME lock+guard-after-lock discipline to store-debit-at-TO-issue, pharmacy-debit-at-TO-issue, and GRN-approve store increment, with deterministic balance-before-batches and uid-ordered multi-line locking. 08a/08b MUST NOT: re-open or CR this lock; reproduce the legacy zero-lock check-then-act race; reproduce the legacy deductBatch empty-catch silent swallow on the LOCKED decrement path (that is a separate N7 defect-fix decision, not licensed by Q4); and MUST NOT treat the §2 "null expiry last" ordering as exact-process — build the lock now but gate the FEFO null-expiry lot-selection semantics on Q8 (HDE) before freezing the parity oracle for which lot is consumed.
decision.ownerActionRequired: None specific to Q4 — the locking decision is already ratified into ADR-0017 and requires no further owner sign-off to build. (Adjacent owner actions remain open but belong to other questions: Q8/HDE owns the FEFO NULLS-LAST vs legacy null-exclusion lot-selection ruling that §2's wording touches; the engagement lead's blanket ADR-ratification posture in docs/adr/README.md still marks all ADRs "Proposed" — if the owner wants the file status reconciled with the charter's "RATIFIED" assertion, that is a one-line doc-status fix, not a Q4 blocker.)

challenge.reason: The decision is engineering-sound but governance-defective on two axes. (1) MIS-STATED RATIFICATION / mis-classification. The §2 mechanism text (lockBalance, lockFefoForDispense, guard-after-lock, deterministic balance-before-batches + uid order, STALE_ENTITY/STOCK_CONTENDED) is quoted accurately against docs/adr/0017-concurrency-and-locking.md. BUT the ADR_PRE_DECIDED classification rests on an unverified 'RATIFIED' claim that the primary source contradicts: ADR-0017 line 2 reads 'Status: Proposed (Architecture phase),' and docs/adr/README.md line 3 states ALL ADRs 'carry a status of Proposed and are pending ratification by the engagement lead before they become binding.' The charter's no-re-litigate rule fires only for a RATIFIED ADR; the specialist concedes the file says 'Proposed' and then overrides it with the workflow preamble's assertion — precisely the un-cross-checked trust the task forbids (the ADR-0009 sequence-name precedent). (2) SMUGGLED DEVIATION. The legacy-extraction source of truth (08-pharmacy-discovery/01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md) is unambiguous that the pessimistic lock is net-new with NO legacy basis: D6 (line 81) calls it 'net-new hardening, not reproduction'; line 108 says 'the pessimistic LOCK around it is the net-new part'; line 161 lists 'pessimistic locking + SELECT-FOR-UPDATE' under 'Items that MUST be RE-SCOPED out of exact-process (CR + engagement-lead approval before any code)'; and Q4 itself (line 172) is an OPEN QUESTION owned by the engagement-lead, phrased 'Approve as a deliberate correctness improvement?' Classifying it ADR_PRE_DECIDED with crNeeded:none and ownerActionRequired:None silently folds an un-approved deviation into the baseline and decides the owner's approval for them. The firm's own precedent (inc-04 CR-05, 04-billing-discovery/11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md) shows a net-new hard gate where legacy only filtered was handled as a scoped CR requiring explicit owner sign-off, not auto-absorbed. (The substantive engineering — pessimistic-on-decrement + @Version-on-transitions split, negative-stock refusal preserved, NULLS-LAST/Q8 and FEFO-on-OTC and the N7 empty-catch correctly carved out — is correct and should be retained once approved.)
challenge.correction: Re-classify Q4 as APPROVED_HARDENING that is GATED on the engagement-lead's Q4 sign-off (treat as OWNER_RELEASE_GATE until that sign-off is recorded) — NOT ADR_PRE_DECIDED. Concretely: (a) Stop asserting ADR-0017 §2 is 'RATIFIED.' On the as-built record ADR-0017 is Status: Proposed (README confirms all ADRs are pending ratification); the citation must read 'build per ADR-0017 §2 design ONCE ADR-0017 is ratified AND the Q4 deviation is approved,' not 'ADR already decides it, no CR.' (b) Set crNeeded to a real CR, e.g. 'CR-08-Q4: net-new PESSIMISTIC_WRITE row lock + @Version split on stock decrement (legacy is zero-lock check-then-act) — correctness hardening preserving the verbatim negative-stock refusal; deviation from observed legacy behaviour requiring written approval before 08a/08b code,' mirroring the inc-04 CR-05 treatment. (c) Set ownerActionRequired to flag (do NOT decide): 'Engagement-lead must approve the Q4 deviation (pessimistic lock + @Version-on-transitions split) AND ratify ADR-0017 before 08a/08b implement the lock; until then the lock is parked, not baseline.' (d) Keep all the correct engineering once approved: pessimistic lock on every decrement path with guard-after-lock; @Version optimistic default with 409 STALE_ENTITY on non-stock transitions; deterministic balance-before-batches + uid-ordered multi-line locking with lock/statement timeout -> 409 STOCK_CONTENDED; the decrement service order verbatim; 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK preserving the legacy refusal message. (e) Retain the explicit exclusions exactly as written: NULLS-LAST lot selection rides on Q8 (HDE), FEFO-on-OTC stays out of baseline (CR), and the legacy deductBatch empty-catch swallow is the separate N7 defect-fix decision — none of these are licensed by Q4.

decision.decision:
SPLIT into three sub-rulings, all empirically re-derived from the I01 source-of-truth (not the planning doc):

(1) CODE COUNT — the "177" figure is the inc-06 phantom recurring and MUST NOT be cited in any inc-08 acceptance criterion. Re-derived from the ratified I01 fixture backend/src/test/resources/fixtures/expected-privilege-codes.txt: exactly 35 DISTINCT privilege codes (26 live @PreAuthorize gates + 9 commented-dead, tagged ACTIVE/DEAD). "177" counts @PreAuthorize annotation SITES across 45 legacy resource classes, not codes. inc-08 adds ZERO new privilege codes to this set; it draws its gate codes only from the seeded 35.

(2) PRESCRIPTION-ALL — does NOT exist as a gate. It is declared as a code-catalogue string in legacy Object_.java:41 ("PRESCRIPTION-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE DELETE APPROVE"), but it is (a) NOT in the seeded/ratified 35-code fixture, and (b) used by ZERO @PreAuthorize on any *Resource.java (grep returns no matches). The legacy clinical dispense flow (issue_medicine) and the entire walk-in OTC PharmacySaleOrder flow are genuinely UNGATED. inc-08 acceptance criteria must NOT cite PRESCRIPTION-ALL as a dispense gate; the clinical-dispense gate code is an OPEN question requiring legacy-analyst/HDE confirmation against the fixture (none exists today).

(3) GRANULARITY (a vs b) — BASELINE = reproduce COARSE gating verbatim (exact-process), with ONE carve-out: closing the default-open posture is APPROVED SECURITY HARDENING. Concretely:
 - 08a/08b reuse the seeded coarse codes exactly: LPO transitions (verify/approve/submit/return/reject) ALL gate on the single LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL (plus -CREATE/-UPDATE on save) — NO distinct -VERIFY/-APPROVE/-SUBMIT/-REJECT codes; GRN gates on GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-ALL/-CREATE/-UPDATE/-APPROVE as legacy; pharmacy admin gates ADMIN-ACCESS and MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE; store ITEM_STOCK-UPDATE; supplier-price SUPPLIER_PRICE_LIST-ALL. The PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL/-CREATE/-UPDATE codes ARE seeded and MAY be applied to the OTC sale flow ONLY as the deny-by-default closure described next (NOT as new SoD).
 - Per-transition SoD codes are NOT built. That is net-new privilege promotion with no legacy basis -> DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR (do NOT build in 08a/08b).
 - Default-open closure IS built: per ADR-0006 Implementation-notes (everything-else authenticated()), inc-08/I01 deny-by-default means every pharmacy/inventory/procurement endpoint requires at minimum authentication, and the legacy anonymous fall-through, commented anyRequest(), literal 'secret' HMAC, and commented token-integrity recheck are NOT ported. Endpoints legacy left UNGATED (whole OTC PharmacySaleOrder flow, GRN list/search, all Supplier endpoints) become authenticated() at minimum. ANY authority code placed on a previously-ungated endpoint beyond bare authenticated() must be drawn from the existing 35 and recorded as hardening with security-architect-owned endpoint->code mapping; it must NOT introduce a new code.
decision.rationale: The question bundles three things that resolve differently, and the dominant ruling is exact-process reproduce. (1) Coarse, no-SoD gating is the verified legacy behaviour AND is the ratified I01 baseline: ADR-0006 says preserve every @PreAuthorize gate with exact codes byte-for-byte; I01 02-security-design.md:116 ratifies that the 9 dead codes are retained for parity and 'do NOT promote any to a live gate without a change request.' Per-transition SoD = promoting/inventing codes = exactly the prohibited move, so it is a DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR, not the baseline. (2) The 177 figure is the documented inc-06 trap (phantom count) recurring; the empirically re-derived count from the ratified fixture is 35, and PRESCRIPTION-ALL is a declared-but-never-gated catalogue string (Object_.java:41) absent from the 35 and from all @PreAuthorize — so the dispense flow is genuinely ungated and no PRESCRIPTION gate may be asserted. (3) The ONE thing that is NOT pure reproduction — closing the default-open posture — is already DECIDED by ADR-0006 (everything-else authenticated()) and is framed by that ADR as a security-defect fix ('defects to correct, not port'), consistent with the charter's APPROVED_HARDENING lane: the legacy anonymous fall-through is a vulnerability (CWE-862 missing authorization / broken access control), not a business PROCESS, so reproducing it would port a vuln, not preserve behaviour. Net classification is EXACT_PROCESS_REPRODUCE because the gating model and code set are reproduced verbatim; the deny-by-default closure rides on an already-ratified ADR and the SoD improvement is explicitly deferred to a CR.

The '177' in ADR-0006 is superseded by the ratified I01 record (docs/delivery/increments/01-iam-discovery/00-build-spec.md:13-15 and 02-security-design.md:17,116; fixture backend/src/test/resources/fixtures/expected-privilege-codes.txt) = 35 distinct codes, 26 live + 9 dead, no promotion without a CR. decision.decision: REPRODUCE THE LEGACY GAP in the exact-process baseline. On the pharmacy<->pharmacy receiving note (PPRN approve_receipt), 08b reproduces PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote (legacy :197-218): it increments the destination aggregate stock (PharmacyMedicine.stock += receivedQty, re-modelled as the StockBalance increment) and writes ONE inbound StockMovement/stock-card IN row only when receivedQty>0 (per N2) -- and it must NOT create destination StockBatch (PharmacyMedicineBatch-equivalent) rows. The legacy batch-creation loop is commented out (:221-232) and is therefore not part of the observed process; 08b reproduces that absence verbatim. Consequence preserved on purpose: transferred-in stock arrives at the destination pharmacy with NO lot/expiry rows, so destination batch sum < destination aggregate balance for any p2p-received stock, and a later FEFO dispense at that pharmacy walks only the batches it does have (silently stranding the un-batched transferred quantity in deductBatch's empty-catch, per N7) while the aggregate negative-stock guard still governs the hard refusal. This is the deliberate, documented divergence from the pharmacy<->STORE path (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl :230-241), which DOES create one destination PharmacyMedicineBatch per StoreToPharmacyBatch and is reproduced as-is in 08b. CLINICAL RISK (state for the register, do not silently fix): inter-pharmacy transferred stock loses expiry/lot traceability at the receiving pharmacy -- expired or soon-to-expire medicine moved between pharmacies cannot be FEFO-selected, recalled by lot, or flagged for expiry at the destination; the receiving pharmacy's batch ledger understates true on-hand and its expiry-management/recall capability is blind to transferred-in stock. This is a genuine patient-safety/medicines-management weakness, but fixing it changes an observable business result and has zero legacy basis, so it does NOT enter the baseline. Raise the fix as a CR (below) for HDE + engagement-owner decision; if approved, the change is small and mirrors the store path (re-parent/snapshot the TO batch rows into destination batches on RN approve). Until that CR is approved and ratified, 08b builds the gap.
decision.rationale: Charter test applied strictly. (1) NOT ADR-pre-decided: ADR-0017 governs only the stock-DECREMENT path (pessimistic lock ordering, FEFO null-expiry-last, negative-stock guard after lock) and document numbering; it is silent on whether a receiving note CREATES destination batch/credit rows. ADR-0003 (persistence) and ADR-0007 (audit) contain no batch/expiry/lot-traceability mandate (0007 governs audit_log rows, not domain batch rows). No ratified ADR decides Q7, so it is not 'build per ADR'. (2) The fix is a tempting clinical-safety improvement with NO legacy basis and it CONTRADICTS observed legacy (the batch loop is deliberately commented out at PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:221-232; confirmed in discovery cite :432-433). Under the charter the baseline answer for an improvement that has no legacy basis / contradicts legacy is reproduce-legacy, and the improvement becomes a DEVIATION. (3) It is NOT APPROVED_HARDENING: that lane is reserved for fixing a security vulnerability the charter would not call 'process' (e.g. the default-open auth posture in N18, or the ADR-0017 oversell race). Loss of expiry traceability is a clinical/data-integrity gap, not a security vulnerability, and creating destination batches changes a business RESULT (new domain rows + changed downstream FEFO behaviour), so it cannot be slipped in as hardening. (4) It is NOT OWNER_RELEASE_GATE: no sign-off-only artifact gates it (unlike Q6's SPTO/PPTO PO gate). It is a substantive behaviour-change decision = CR. The 'modern design' carve-out (double->BigDecimal, ULID, StockBalance/StockMovement re-model) permits re-modelling HOW batches are stored but does not authorize CREATING batch rows on a path where legacy creates none -- that is process, not data-type. The discovery doc already (correctly) lists 'p2p destination-batch creation' among items that MUST be re-scoped out of exact-process via CR (line 161) and flags it as N6.
decision.adrCitation: none -- ADRs silent on destination-batch creation. ADR-0017 (concurrency-and-locking) §Decision-2 + Implementation-notes governs only the decrement path (lock -> guard -> decrement balance -> FEFO-decrement batches -> stock_card row); it does not address receiving-note batch credit. ADR-0003 and ADR-0007 contain no batch/expiry/lot-traceability requirement. So Q7 is not pre-decided by any ratified ADR.
decision.crNeeded: CR-Q7: Create destination StockBatch (lot/expiry) rows on pharmacy<->pharmacy receiving-note approval to restore inter-pharmacy expiry/lot traceability (mirror the pharmacy<->store RN path) -- clinical-safety DEVIATION from legacy, requires HDE + engagement-owner approval before 08b code.
decision.buildImpact: 08a: none (clinical/OTC dispense and stock-core primitives unaffected; this is a transfer concern). 08b MUST BUILD (exact-process baseline): PPRN approve_receipt increments destination StockBalance + writes ONE IN StockMovement/stock-card row only when receivedQty>0, and MUST NOT create any destination StockBatch rows for the p2p path. 08b MUST NOT 'helpfully' add the destination batch loop, and MUST NOT auto-derive balance := sum(batch.remainingQty) on the destination (doing so would mask the legacy desync and break parity). Keep the pharmacy<->STORE PGRN path creating destination batches (one per StoreToPharmacyBatch) -- the asymmetry is intentional and is itself a parity assertion. Parity/QA: golden-master test must assert that after a p2p RN, destination aggregate balance increased by receivedQty while destination batch-row COUNT is unchanged (batch sum < balance), and must NOT expect a destination batch or an error. Edge-case register (HDE deliverable): add an entry 'p2p transfer loses destination lot/expiry traceability' tied to CR-Q7. Audit (ADR-0007): the RN approval still emits its audit_log row regardless. If/when CR-Q7 is approved+ratified, 08b switches to mirroring the store path (snapshot/re-parent TO batch rows into destination StockBatch rows on RN approve) and the parity oracle is updated accordingly.
decision.ownerActionRequired: Engagement/product owner (with HDE) must decide whether to approve CR-Q7. Only the owner can authorize this deviation from the exact-process baseline; the HDE recommends raising it because the gap is a real medicines-management/patient-safety weakness (no destination expiry tracking or lot recall for inter-pharmacy stock), but per the charter the HDE does not unilaterally build the fix. Until the owner approves CR-Q7, 08b ships the reproduced gap. (No separate release-gate sign-off artifact is involved -- this is a scope/CR approval, distinct from Q6's SPTO/PPTO PO release gate.)

decision.decision: SPLIT THE QUESTION INTO THREE PARTS — they resolve differently. (1) DATED-LOT FEFO ORDER + NULL-EXPIRY HANDLING (clinical issue_medicine path + p2p/p2s transfer-issue paths): BUILD PER ADR-0017 §2 — lockFefoForDispense orders batches "expiry ascending, NULL EXPIRY LAST". This is ratified and OVERRIDES the legacy silent null-expiry exclusion. Legacy getEarlierBatch (PatientResource.java:3338-3376, verified) drops null-expiry lots entirely whenever any dated lot exists (they can be stranded forever); ADR-0017 instead dispenses dated lots first then null-expiry lots last. 08a builds NULLS LAST. (2) SECONDARY/TIEBREAK SORT: pin to id ASC — order is (expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST, id ASC). This is consistent with ADR-0017 §2 (which is silent on the tiebreak) and with the legacy FIFO-by-lowest-id intent (legacy uses lowest-id, verified in the no-expiry branch at 3364-3373); making it the explicit deterministic secondary key is a determinism hardening, not a behaviour change. (3) FEFO-ON-OTC: do NOT build. Legacy give_medicine has deductBatch COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:6317, verified) plus a commented destination-batch block (6298-6309) and a "//review this section later" author note — OTC decrements aggregate PharmacyMedicine.stock + writes a PharmacyStockCard OUT row only, NEVER consumes PharmacyMedicineBatch. Applying the unified FEFO to OTC is a DEVIATION with no legacy basis → requires an APPROVED CR before any code. 08a's OTC dispense reproduces aggregate-only decrement; the shared FEFO primitive is wired to clinical/transfer paths only, NOT to PharmacySaleOrder. MANDATORY DISCLOSURE: the NULLS LAST decision is an intended, ADR-sanctioned behaviour change — a previously-stranded null-expiry lot will now be dispensed (after all dated lots). This must be recorded in the 08a behaviour-change/disclosure register and a golden-master parity exception noted (legacy would never have selected that lot while a dated lot existed).
decision.rationale: The core of Q8 (the FEFO ordering and null-expiry placement on the dispense/transfer-issue path) is already decided by ADR-0017 §2, which the binding charter lists as a RATIFIED fact: lockFefoForDispense reads stock_batch rows "in expiry-ascending order, null expiry last," with the negative-stock guard after the pessimistic lock. The ADR frames the whole stock-decrement change as "preserve the legacy negative-stock REFUSAL process, make it race-safe — a correctness improvement, not a behaviour change," and explicitly adopts the prior-attempt "FEFO batch dispensing with pessimistic lock" pattern verbatim. NULLS LAST is therefore the ratified answer and is NOT re-litigated; it deliberately supersedes the legacy silent exclusion (NULLS LAST dispenses stranded lots last rather than never). Clinically this is the safer behaviour: a lot with no recorded expiry should not be permanently un-dispensable inventory, and consuming it only after all dated stock is exhausted is consistent with FEFO intent while preventing dead stock — but the ratified basis here is the ADR, not a fresh clinical judgement. The id-ASC tiebreak is a determinism choice the ADR is silent on; pinning it removes nondeterminism in the legacy (which relied on iteration/insert order for equal-expiry dated lots) and matches the legacy lowest-id intent, so it is safe to fix as part of the same build with no separate CR. FEFO-on-OTC is categorically different: legacy never consumed batches on OTC (verified commented-out at 6317), so unifying FEFO onto OTC is net-new behaviour with no legacy basis and falls under the charter's DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR rule — but since the dated-FEFO question proper is ADR-decided, the overall question classifies ADR_PRE_DECIDED with an explicit carved-out CR for the OTC sub-part. Verified the two load-bearing legacy facts directly in source (getEarlierBatch 3338-3376; OTC commented deductBatch 6317) and confirmed ADR-0017 §2 text verbatim.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0017 §2 (Pessimistic row lock for stock decrement) — "lockFefoForDispense(...) (batches in expiry-ascending order, null expiry last)"; supported by ADR-0017 §Decision intro ("adopts the prior-attempt FEFO batch dispensing with pessimistic lock pattern verbatim") and §Implementation notes ("FEFO-decrement batches"). ADR-0017 §2 is SILENT on the secondary/tiebreak sort key — id ASC is an in-engagement determinism pin, not an ADR clause. ADR-0009 §3 governs the NUMERIC(19,6) qty precision of the batch quantities consumed. NOTE: ADR-0017 file header still reads Status "Proposed (Architecture phase)" — the binding charter lists §2 as RATIFIED; flag the doc-status line for housekeeping update, it does not change the decision.
decision.crNeeded: CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC: "Apply unified FEFO batch consumption + lot-traceability to walk-in OTC PharmacySaleOrder dispense (legacy deductBatch commented out — net-new, do NOT build in 08a baseline)." (Optional second item, owner discretion: CR to disclose/accept the NULLS LAST stranded-lot dispense as a deliberate golden-master parity exception — though this is already ADR-sanctioned, so it is a disclosure entry rather than a deviation CR.)
decision.buildImpact: 08a MUST build: a single shared FEFO primitive lockFefoForDispense ordering stock_batch by (expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST, id ASC) under @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) per ADR-0017 §2, with the negative-stock guard AFTER the lock; consumed on the clinical dispense (NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN, all-or-nothing) path and writing lot-trace rows (the PrescriptionBatch equivalent — do not silently drop legacy lot traceability). 08b MUST reuse the SAME ordered, locked FEFO primitive on p2p and p2s transfer-issue (source-debit at TO.issue) — the p2p variant must still NOT create destination batches in the baseline (that is Q7's separate CR). 08a/08b MUST NOT: (a) reproduce the legacy silent null-expiry EXCLUSION (it is overridden by NULLS LAST); (b) leave the equal-expiry tiebreak to iteration/insert order (pin id ASC); (c) wire the FEFO primitive into the OTC give_medicine path — OTC dispense in 08a decrements aggregate stock + writes a stock-card OUT row ONLY, with NO batch consumption and NO lot-trace rows, exactly as legacy (deductBatch stays "not invoked"). Parity harness: add a NULLS-LAST behaviour-change test (stranded null-expiry lot now dispensed last) flagged as an intended divergence, and an OTC test asserting batch tables are untouched by an OTC sale.
decision.ownerActionRequired: Owner (engagement-lead) action 1: approve/raise CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC before any FEFO-on-OTC code is written — until then 08a builds OTC as aggregate-only (the safe baseline; no owner action blocks 08a from proceeding on the OTC-as-legacy path). Owner action 2 (disclosure, not a gate): acknowledge in the engagement behaviour-change register that NULLS LAST dispenses a previously-stranded null-expiry lot — this is ADR-sanctioned so it needs sign-off as a disclosed parity exception, not a new decision. The NULLS LAST / id ASC dispense ordering itself needs NO owner sign-off (ADR-0017 §2 already ratifies it). challenge.reason:
The build TARGETS are correct and the load-bearing legacy facts are all verified in source: getEarlierBatch (PatientResource.java:3338-3376) silently excludes null-expiry lots whenever any dated lot exists and uses lowest-id in the no-expiry branch (3363-3373); OTC give_medicine has deductBatch commented out (6317) plus a commented destination-batch block (6298-6309) and the "review this section later" note (6313) — so OTC is aggregate-stock + stock-card-OUT only, never batch-consuming. The specialist correctly carved FEFO-ON-OTC out as a CR (CR-08), correctly pinned the id-ASC tiebreak as an ADR-silent determinism choice, and correctly cited ADR-0009 §3 (NUMERIC(19,6) qty) — that cite is accurate and the ADR-0009 sequence-name drift (seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no in the ADR vs as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no in V13, verified lines 33/37) is not load-bearing for Q8. So Axes 1 and 2 largely pass: no ADR mis-citation, and the highest-risk deviation (FEFO-on-OTC) is properly CR-gated, not smuggled.

The defect is on Axis 3 (classification + owner action). The specialist classified Q8 as fully ADR_PRE_DECIDED and concluded the NULLS LAST dispense-ordering "needs NO owner sign-off" — reducing it to a disclosure entry. That overreaches for two reasons. (1) ADR-0017 is a CONCURRENCY/locking ADR; it adopts the "null expiry last" wording verbatim from the prior-attempt FEFO-pessimistic-lock lesson (prior-attempt-lessons.md:166-167) as a race-safety mechanism, and its own "Exact-process impact" section asserts "None on business outcomes ... a correctness improvement, not a behaviour change." But the verified legacy semantics (and the firm's own reconciliation D7/N8) establish NULLS LAST is NOT behaviour-neutral — it changes WHICH lot dispenses (a previously-stranded null-expiry lot is now consumed after dated stock). An ADR cannot pre-ratify a behaviour change it explicitly disclaims; the ratified scope is "make the negative-stock refusal race-safe," not "change FEFO lot-selection policy." (2) The firm already routed exactly this to the owner: the inc-08 reconciliation lists Q8 in OPEN QUESTIONS with owner = HDE — "Preserve the exclusion quirk, or fix to NULLS LAST (changes which lot dispenses)?" — and there is NO inc-08 DECISIONS-RATIFIED doc closing it (the ratified-decisions series stops at inc-05). Selecting a no-recorded-expiry medicine lot for dispense is a clinical-safety call assigned to the HDE; the charter forbids resolving a legacy-behaviour ambiguity by applying "best practice" that overrides legacy process. The specialist's rationale even makes the clinical argument ("a lot with no recorded expiry should not be permanently un-dispensable") — which is the HDE's call to make, not the specialist's. Telling the owner "no sign-off required" silently decides the one thing the reconciliation flagged for them.
challenge.correction: Keep the build targets but downgrade the classification and fix the owner-action gating. (1) Reclassify Q8 as PARTIALLY ADR-decided + DEVIATION_NEEDS_OWNER_SIGN-OFF, not flatly ADR_PRE_DECIDED: ADR-0017 §2 ratifies the FEFO-under-pessimistic-lock MECHANISM and the dated-lot expiry-ascending order; it does NOT by itself ratify the clinical behaviour change of dispensing previously-stranded null-expiry lots, because the ADR explicitly disclaims any business-outcome change. (2) The NULLS LAST decision REQUIRES an explicit HDE sign-off (Q8 is an open HDE-owned question with no inc-08 ratification), recorded as an approved, deliberate behaviour change with a golden-master parity exception — the disclosure-register entry is necessary but NOT sufficient; it must not be presented to the owner as "no sign-off required." (3) Until the HDE signs off, the 08a baseline must reproduce the VERIFIED legacy null-expiry EXCLUSION (dated lots only; null-expiry lots stranded), since that is the confirmed exact-process behaviour; NULLS LAST is wired in only once HDE approval lands. Unchanged and upheld: the shared lockFefoForDispense ordered (expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST, id ASC) under PESSIMISTIC_WRITE with the negative-stock guard after the lock (per ADR-0017 §2) is the approved TARGET; the id-ASC tiebreak needs no separate CR; FEFO is wired to clinical-dispense + p2p/p2s transfer-issue only; OTC stays aggregate-only with FEFO-on-OTC behind CR-08; lot-trace (PrescriptionBatch-equivalent) rows must not be silently dropped. Also flag for housekeeping: ADR-0017 header still reads "Proposed" though the charter lists §2 as RATIFIED.

decision.decision: REPRODUCE LEGACY VERBATIM as the 08a baseline, with two normalization items parked as change requests. Concretely: (1) NUMBERING — keep OTC order number output `PSO/{id}` (forward slash, raw PK suffix, NO date, NO Formater, NO `-`, NO zero-pad) and keep BOTH divergent customer-number formats by entry path: `PCST{id}` on the savePharmacySaleOrder path and `PCST/{year}/{id}` on the createPharmacyCustomer path. These are NOT covered by the ADR-0009 prefix/sequence table and must NOT be coerced into `{PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}` nor into the shared DocumentNumberService. They may use the same defect-free single-insert mechanism ADR-0009 already blessed (the `{id}` suffix = the row's own PK; do NOT reproduce the legacy save-then-renumber double-save), but the OUTPUT shape `PSO/{id}` / `PCST{id}` / `PCST/{year}/{id}` is reproduced exactly. (2) PRICING/PAYMENT — OTC bill amount stays flat `Medicine.price * qty` (Money NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP per ADR-0009 §2), with NO insurance/co-pay/discount/waiver lookup; route the OTC charge through `billing::api.recordClinicalCharge` with `ChargeRequest.paymentType` set to the literal CASH and the incoming payment-mode value DELIBERATELY IGNORED, bills hung on the GENERAL dummy patient. As a direct, intended consequence, any "OTC revenue by payment mode" report (BILL-5 / ADR-0010 collection-by-payment-method) renders all-CASH in the exact-process baseline; 08a must build it that way and 08b/reporting must not invent multi-mode OTC breakdowns. Two CRs are raised for the tempting improvements (numbering normalization; honoring real payment mode); neither is built in 08a/08b until engagement-lead approves at the phase gate.
decision.rationale: Q9 splits into two sub-questions and BOTH resolve to reproduce-legacy under the charter, because no ratified ADR governs either. (A) Numbering: ADR-0009 §5 reproduces document-number FORMATS verbatim and §122-137 gives a COMPLETE prefix/sequence table — but that table lists only GRN/LPO/PCN/PRL/PPRN/PSR/PPR/SPTO/PPTO/PGRN/USR/MR. PSO and PCST are absent, and the legacy schemes are deliberately NOT the `PREFIX{yyyyMMdd}-{id}` pattern (no date, no `-`, no Formater) — N17 confirms they must not be coerced into it. ADR-0009 is therefore SILENT on OTC numbering: it neither decides nor blesses normalization, so the charter default (reproduce verbatim) governs and normalization is a deviation requiring a CR. Reproducing the legacy OUTPUT shape while dropping the save-then-renumber double-save is consistent with ADR-0009's own stance (format reproduced, double-save rejected as an implementation defect, §67/§84) and is NOT itself a deviation. (B) Pricing/payment: no ADR decides OTC pricing. ADR-0008 defines a `ChargeRequest.paymentType` field (the seam CAN carry a real value) but mandates nothing about what the OTC path passes. Legacy hardcodes CASH and ignores the incoming value (PatientServiceImpl.java:3342; flat `Medicine.price*qty` at :3421-3442/:3395-3442). Honoring the real payment mode is a tempting improvement with no legacy basis and one that CHANGES an observable output (the by-payment-mode report) — squarely DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR, not APPROVED_HARDENING (it fixes no security vulnerability; the charter would not call payment-mode reporting fidelity a security fix). It is NOT OWNER_RELEASE_GATE because, unlike SPTO/PPTO (ADR-0009 §6) or business scope sign-offs, no ADR clause gates these on the product owner; the BA action is to raise the CRs and the engagement-lead approves them at the phase gate, which is the standard deviation path, hence the classification is DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR rather than OWNER_RELEASE_GATE.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0009 §5 and §122-137 (Prefix table) — SILENT on PSO/PCST: the verbatim-format rule and the complete prefix/sequence table cover only the date-stamped document types; OTC numbers are deliberately excluded (confirmed N17). ADR-0009 §2 governs the Money scale (NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP) the flat OTC amount is persisted at. ADR-0008 line 69 defines `ChargeRequest.paymentType` (the billing seam) but does NOT decide the OTC paymentType value. ADR-0010 line 66 defines the collection-by-payment-method report whose OTC slice is all-CASH under the baseline. No ADR decides OTC pricing or payment-mode capture — ADRs silent on the deviation question.
decision.crNeeded: TWO CRs: (1) "CR: Normalize OTC numbering — unify PharmacyCustomer to a single PCST format and/or migrate PSO/PCST onto the shared DocumentNumberService date-sequence scheme (deviates from legacy `PSO/{id}` + dual `PCST` output)"; (2) "CR: Honor real OTC payment mode at billing (stop hardcoding paymentType=CASH; persist incoming payment mode) to enable a true OTC-revenue-by-payment-mode report — supersedes/implements BILL-5"
