challenge.reason:
Axis-2/Axis-1 defect in the NUMBERING sub-question (A). The PRICING/PAYMENT sub-question (B) is charter-compliant and well-cited: flat Money(price).multiply(qty) at NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP (ADR-0009 §2), paymentType hardcoded CASH with the incoming mode ignored, GENERAL dummy patient, and the intended all-CASH consequence for the ADR-0010 collection-by-payment-method report, with the real-payment-mode improvement correctly parked as a CR — all verified against legacy PatientServiceImpl.java:3342 (setPaymentType("CASH")) and :3422/:3424 (price*qty, status UNPAID, no insurance/co-pay). No ADR pre-decides OTC pricing; DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR for that improvement is right.

The numbering sub-question is the problem. The decision instructs 08a to BUILD the output `PSO/{id}` / `PCST{id}` / `PCST/{year}/{id}` where, verbatim, "the {id} suffix = the row's own PK," while also "dropping the save-then-renumber double-save." That is (a) internally contradictory and (b) contradicts a ratified ADR. Verified legacy (PatientServiceImpl.java:3344-3354 PSO, :3304-3314 PCST{id}, :3249 PCST/{year}/{id}) embeds the auto-IDENTITY PK, which is only knowable AFTER the first insert — so reproducing "suffix = the row's own PK" REQUIRES the save→read-PK→update (the double-save the decision rejects) AND re-exposes the surrogate id. ADR-0014 §1 is MANDATORY and "absolute and non-negotiable": id MUST NOT appear in any DTO, response body, URL, or any client-facing value — and a document number printed/returned in the API is exactly that. The firm has ALREADY ruled on this identical situation: legacy MRNO used MRNO/{year}/{patient.getId()} (PK suffix), and CR-02 (RATIFIED, 03-registration-discovery/11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:8) decoupled it to nextval('seq_mrno') precisely because "ADR-0014 §1 hides id," explicitly calling the PK-vs-sequence suffix an OBSERVABLE CHANGE requiring engagement-lead approval (02-extract-mrno-registration-bill.md:35). V13 confirms NO seq_pso_no / seq_pcst_no exists, so a sequence route additionally needs a new V39+ migration. The decision treats the PK-suffix-vs-sequence provenance as a free verbatim baseline when it is a CR-gated decoupling decision the firm has already established as such.

Other claims hold up: ADR-0009 §5 and the §122-137 prefix table genuinely omit PSO/PCST (silent — confirmed), so N17's "do not coerce into {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}" and "not on the shared DocumentNumberService" are correct; ADR-0008 line 69 and ADR-0010 line 66 are cited accurately. No ADR-name drift on the cited clauses (unlike the seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no vs as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no defect, which is not in play here). Classification DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR is right for the two parked improvements; the gap is that the BASELINE the decision tells 08a to build is not a clean verbatim reproduction and silently picks the ADR-0014-violating PK-exposure branch.
challenge.correction: Keep sub-question B exactly as written (flat price*qty at NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP, paymentType=CASH literal, incoming mode ignored, GENERAL dummy patient, all-CASH ADR-0010 OTC slice, real-payment-mode improvement parked as CR). FIX sub-question A: remove the instruction that "the {id} suffix = the row's own PK" — that branch violates ADR-0014 §1 (mandatory hidden-id) and is internally inconsistent with "no double-save." Reproduce the OUTPUT FORMAT STRINGS verbatim (PSO/{n}, PCST{n}, PCST/{year}/{n} — forward slash, no date, no Formater, no zero-pad, and KEEP the two divergent customer formats by entry path) but treat the numeric suffix PROVENANCE as the same CR-02-style decoupling already ratified for MRNO: substitute a dedicated PostgreSQL sequence (new seq_pso_no / seq_pcst_no via a V39+ migration; one atomic nextval, single-insert, no double-save) for the surrogate PK, because ADR-0014 §1 forbids exposing id in any client-facing value. Because this decouples the visible number from the row PK (an observable change — PSO/5 will no longer imply PK 5), it is NOT a silent baseline item: raise it as a numbering-provenance CR to engagement-lead exactly as CR-02 did for MRNO, and keep it OUTSIDE the shared date-stamped DocumentNumberService (ADR-0009 is silent on OTC). The QA/parity acceptance criteria must assert the verbatim format strings and the dual PCST shapes, NOT a PK-equals-suffix value. This makes THREE CRs total: (1) numbering-suffix provenance/decoupling (PK->dedicated seq, ADR-0014-forced, MRNO/CR-02 precedent) PLUS the optional unification of the dual PCST format; (2) honor real OTC payment mode; (3) — note CRs (1) and the normalization-to-DocumentNumberService idea may be packaged together, but the ADR-0014-forced seq decoupling is the part that must be flagged even to merely reproduce the format legally. Do not implement any of these until engagement-lead approves at the phase gate.

decision.decision: EXACT-PROCESS BASELINE: preserve the legacy preview-then-supply contract for PPR (PharmacyToPharmacy RO) and PSR (PharmacyToStore RO) VERBATIM. The client previews a number and posts it back on create; the server does NOT call setNo() and does NOT overwrite the supplied number. This matches legacy (discovery I/D15: RO numbers are client-supplied; only TO/RN/GRN/LPO are server-assigned). DO NOT move PPR/PSR to a server-authoritative DocumentNumberService.next()-inside-the-insert-tx as part of the inc-08 exact-process build. Moving them is a behaviour change to the request contract (drops the client-supplied request_no field, changes the Angular preview-and-post-back flow, changes the OpenAPI request body) and is therefore a DEVIATION, not exact-process. I am raising a CR for the server-authoritative variant, but the go/no-go is an engagement-owner call (Q10 owner = engagement-lead; it is on the re-scope-needs-approval list) — I am NOT deciding it for them. CONCURRENCY-SAFETY NUANCE THAT STILL APPLIES NOW: even while preserving the client-supplied contract, the preview endpoint MUST source its candidate number from the same backing stream as ADR-0009 §5 (seq_ppr_no / seq_psr_no) rather than recomputing MAX(id)+1; and the create path MUST enforce a DB UNIQUE constraint on the RO `no` column so a duplicate/colliding client-supplied number is hard-rejected (409/422), not silently accepted. That closes the duplicate-number risk WITHOUT changing the request contract and stays inside ADR-0009 §5's "make numbering concurrency-safe" mandate. If the preview cannot be made authoritative against the sequence without a contract change, the duplicate-defense reduces to the UNIQUE constraint + retry, which is sufficient and contract-preserving.
decision.rationale: ADR-0009 §5 does NOT pre-decide this. §5 decides HOW a number is generated concurrency-safely (one dedicated PG SEQUENCE per type — seq_ppr_no and seq_psr_no are explicitly listed — single-insert, MAX(id)+1 race killed, double-save defect dropped, format PREFIX{yyyyMMdd EAT}-{seq} reproduced verbatim). It does NOT decide WHO ORIGINATES the number. The ADR's own Context describes legacy numbering only as server-side Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix, MAX(id)+1); it never recorded that PPR/PSR are client-supplied preview-and-post-back (that is an inc-08 discovery finding, D15). A ratified ADR cannot have decided a contract it never observed, so §5's mandate cannot be stretched to cover converting a client-originated contract into a server-originated one. Under the charter, a tempting improvement that CONTRADICTS observed legacy behaviour (here: the request contract) has reproduce-legacy as its baseline, and the improvement becomes a deviation requiring a written, APPROVED CR before implementation. This is NOT APPROVED_HARDENING: that lane is reserved for fixing a security vulnerability the charter would not call "process" (e.g., N18 default-open auth). Client-supplied document numbers are a data-integrity/contract concern, not an auth vulnerability — and the duplicate-number risk is fully mitigable WITHOUT a contract change via a DB UNIQUE constraint on the RO `no` (which ADR-0009 §5's concurrency-safety mandate already supports). So the duplicate-number argument does not force the contract change; it only motivates the constraint, which the baseline build must include anyway.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0009 §5 (numbering made concurrency-safe; seq_ppr_no, seq_psr_no listed) and §6 + prefix table (PPR, PSR prefixes verbatim) govern the FORMAT and concurrency-safety; ADR-0009 is SILENT on number ORIGINATION (client-supplied vs server-assigned) for PPR/PSR — its Context only models server-side MAX(id)+1, never the preview-and-post-back contract. No ADR decides Q10.
decision.crNeeded: CR-Q10: Make PPR/PSR request-order numbers server-authoritative (drop client-supplied request_no; assign via DocumentNumberService.next() inside the insert tx) — aligns all 8 doc types and removes the client-trust surface; behaviour change to the RO request contract and Angular preview flow. Owner = engagement-lead.
decision.buildImpact: 08b (store/procurement/transfers, which owns PPR and PSR) MUST: (1) reproduce the client-supplied preview-then-supply contract for PPR and PSR — keep request_no in the create request body; server persists the supplied value and does NOT setNo() or override it; (2) ADD a DB UNIQUE constraint on the PPR `no` and PSR `no` columns and surface a hard 409/422 (DUPLICATE_DOCUMENT_NUMBER or STALE_ENTITY-style) on collision — this is the contract-preserving defense against the duplicate-number risk and is in scope NOW under ADR-0009 §5; (3) back the PPR/PSR PREVIEW endpoint with the seq_ppr_no/seq_psr_no sequences and the verbatim PREFIX{yyyyMMdd EAT}-{seq} format (do NOT recompute MAX(id)+1 client- or server-side); (4) keep the 6 server-assigned types (GRN/LPO/SPTO/PPTO/PPRN/PGRN) on DocumentNumberService.next()-inside-insert-tx per ADR-0009 §5 as already scoped. 08b MUST NOT: build server-authoritative PPR/PSR (no DocumentNumberService.next() inside the RO insert tx, no removal of request_no) unless and until CR-Q10 is approved by engagement-lead. The parity harness must test PPR/PSR create from a client-supplied number (not server-assigned).
decision.ownerActionRequired: engagement-lead must decide CR-Q10 (whether to deviate to server-authoritative PPR/PSR numbering). Until that approval, the exact-process baseline (client-supplied preview-then-supply + UNIQUE-constraint hardening) stands and 08b builds to it. The solution-architect does not decide the deviation; the owner does.

challenge.reason:
Verified on all three adversarial axes against the actual ADR text, the as-built migrations, and the legacy discovery evidence.

AXIS 1 (ADR contradiction) — NONE. I read ADR-0009 in full (docs/adr/0009-money-and-numbering.md). §5 (line 34-37) and the Implementation-notes prefix table (lines 122-138) decide only the FORMAT (PREFIX{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}, PPR/PSR prefixes verbatim) and the concurrency-safe GENERATION mechanism (one PG sequence per type, MAX(id)+1 race killed, single-insert no double-save). The ADR Context (lines 12-14) models legacy numbering ONLY as server-side Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix, MAX(id)+1); it never recorded the preview-and-post-back / client-supplied contract. Q10's claim that ADR-0009 is SILENT on number ORIGINATION (who originates the number) is therefore accurate — a ratified ADR cannot have pre-decided a contract it never observed. I also cross-checked the as-built per the reconciliation warning: seq_ppr_no (V13:49) and seq_psr_no (V13:53) exist and match ADR-0009 §5 verbatim. The known real-drift (seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no in the ADR vs as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no in V13:33,37) is confined to the TWO TO streams; it does NOT touch the PPR/PSR RO sequences Q10 governs, so Q10's seq_ppr_no/seq_psr_no citation is correct against the as-built and does not inherit that drift.

AXIS 2 (smuggled deviation) — NONE; the decision actually BLOCKS one. The legacy baseline is confirmed by discovery 00-discovery-raw.json: PPR (PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:372-381) and PSR (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:368-375) are CLIENT-SUPPLIED — save() never calls setNo() server-side; the only setNo() calls (114/329, 109/324) are in commented-out blocks; the persisted no comes from the client payload previewed via requestRequestOrderNo(). D15 is classified DRIFT: server-authoritative is True for GRN/LPO/TO/RN (6 types), FALSE for PPR/PSR. Crucially, the current inc-08 build doc (08-pharmacy-inventory-procurement.md item 5, lines 117-126) currently implies ALL doc types — including PPR/PSR — are server-assigned via DocumentNumberService.next(); Q10 correctly catches and fences that latent drift. The UNIQUE-constraint defense Q10 mandates for the baseline is itself legacy-faithful: discovery (domain/PharmacyToStoreRO.java:48-50) confirms legacy already carries @Column(unique=true) on no as 'the only collision guard', so reproducing it is exact-process, not net-new scope, and it sits comfortably inside ADR-0009 §5's make-numbering-concurrency-safe mandate. Q10 explicitly forbids building the server-authoritative variant (no setNo() inside the RO insert tx, no removal of request_no) until CR-Q10 is approved.

AXIS 3 (classification & owner action) — CORRECT. DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR is the right lane: converting a client-originated contract to server-originated contradicts observed legacy behaviour, so under the charter the baseline is reproduce-legacy and the improvement is a deviation requiring a written APPROVED CR first. The specialist correctly declines to self-decide and routes go/no-go to the engagement-lead (Q10 owner = engagement-lead, on the re-scope-needs-approval list per 01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md:178). The APPROVED_HARDENING rejection is also sound — client-supplied document numbers are a data-integrity/contract concern, not an auth vulnerability, and the duplicate-number risk is fully mitigable without a contract change via the legacy-faithful UNIQUE constraint, so the risk does not force the contract change.

No charter violation, no ADR mis-citation, nothing built beyond verified legacy behaviour without a CR.

decision.decision: PROMOTE DocumentNumberService + DocumentType to the SHARED KERNEL (com.otapp.hmis.shared.documentnumber), and DECOUPLE the sequence read from any module's domain repository. Concretely: (1) Create shared.documentnumber.DocumentNumberService (interface) + DocumentNumberServiceImpl, with DocumentType as an enum in the same package carrying both prefix() and sequenceName() (e.g. PCN("PCN","seq_pcn_no"), GRN("GRN","seq_grn_no"), LPO("LPO","seq_lpo_no"), SPTO("SPTO","seq_spto_no"), PPTO("PPTO","seq_ppto_no"), PGRN("PGRN","seq_pgrn_no"), PPRN("PPRN","seq_pprn_no"), PPR("PPR","seq_ppr_no"), PSR("PSR","seq_psr_no")), exactly per ADR-0009 §5 / §impl-notes. (2) Replace the per-repository nextPcnNo() coupling: the impl issues one generic SELECT nextval(:seqName) via EntityManager against a fixed allow-list of the seq names DECLARED on the DocumentType enum (the enum is the only source of sequence names; the native string is never built from caller input), formats prefix+yyyyMMdd(EAT)+'-'+seq UNPADDED with the test-overridable Clock, and keeps billing's existing seq_pcn_no behaviour byte-identical. (3) billing.application's DocumentType + DocumentNumberService(+Impl) are DELETED and CreditNoteService rewired to the shared bean; PatientCreditNoteRepository.nextPcnNo() may be removed or retained as dead — engineering choice, no behaviour change. Because shared is already @ApplicationModule(Type.OPEN), pharmacy, inventory and billing all depend on it WITHOUT a new allowed-dependency edge and WITHOUT any inventory->billing or pharmacy->billing edge being introduced for numbering. The md_document_types prefix REGISTRY stays in masterdata (it is reference/config data, correctly owned there and already exposed via masterdata::lookup); the numbering MECHANISM (sequence allocation + format) is the cross-cutting concern that belongs in shared. The DocumentType enum's prefixes must stay consistent with the masterdata-seeded rows (both already say SPTO/PPTO per CR-10) — an IT asserts enum prefix == md_document_types prefix for each kind to prevent drift.
decision.rationale: The reconciliation doc itself flags Q11 as a PURE ARCHITECTURE decision with no legacy-process impact, and the charter routes pure-architecture module-ownership calls to the solution-architect — so this is decided now, not deferred. The document-number FORMAT, the per-type sequences, and the SPTO/PPTO collision fix are ALREADY ratified (ADR-0009 §5/§6, CR-09, CR-10, seeded in V13/V14); nothing about business behaviour, output strings, or numbering scheme is touched by this decision. Only ownership/placement of the mechanism is open, and ADR-0009 deliberately specified DocumentType as carrying prefix+sequenceName with a generic nextval(sequenceName) impl — i.e. a mechanism that needs NO module's domain repository, confirming it was always meant to be cross-cutting. The current billing placement is an accident of inc-04 (PCN was the first and only document type then) and creates two boundary hazards under ADR-0008 §6: extending the billing-owned enum forces inventory->billing and pharmacy->billing edges purely for numbering (coupling the procurement/transfer money flow to the cashiering module with no domain reason), and it keeps the impl bound to billing.domain.PatientCreditNoteRepository. Promotion to shared resolves both: shared is Type.OPEN so every module already depends on it edge-free, ApplicationModules.verify() stays green with NO new edges and NO cycle risk, and the legacy MAX(id)+1 race remains fixed by the same atomic nextval. The prefix REGISTRY correctly stays in masterdata (reference data); separating registry-as-config (masterdata) from allocation-mechanism (shared) is the clean split. This does not weaken RBAC, does not touch PHI audit, and preserves every ratified output, so no CR and no owner gate are required.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0008 §1 (no module imports another's @Entity; reference foreign aggregates by uid; api-only cross-module) and §6 (allowed-dependency graph: every module -> shared; cycles forbidden) govern the boundary; ADR-0009 §5 + Implementation notes ("DocumentType enum carries prefix and sequenceName"; generic next(type) = nextval(sequenceName)) define the mechanism; ADR-0014 §1/§2 (shared kernel placement, package-by-context) consistent. ADRs are SILENT on the explicit owning module for the service -- so this addendum assigns it; it does not contradict any ratified ADR text.
decision.crNeeded: none (no legacy-process deviation; output format/prefixes/sequences unchanged and already ratified)
decision.buildImpact: PREREQUISITE (architecture, before 08a/08b, owned by solution-architect): MUST build a shared.documentnumber package containing DocumentNumberService + Impl + DocumentType enum (prefix + sequenceName per ADR-0009 §5); the impl reads sequences via a generic EntityManager nextval keyed off the enum-declared sequenceName (NOT off any domain repository); migrate billing off its local copy and rewire CreditNoteService. 08a MUST consume the shared service for OTC/dispense-side numbering (note: PSO/PCST are NOT DocumentType-managed per N17 -- they keep verbatim 'PSO/'+id and dual PCST formats and MUST NOT be coerced into the shared service). 08b MUST consume the shared service for GRN/LPO/SPTO/PPTO/PGRN/PPRN and (only if Q10 resolves to server-authoritative) PPR/PSR. 08a/08b MUST NOT add an inventory->billing or pharmacy->billing allowed-dependency edge for numbering, MUST NOT add a second/parallel DocumentNumberService in any module, and MUST NOT re-create the per-document-type sequences (already seeded V13; build starts at V39 per the reconciliation doc). An IT MUST assert DocumentType enum prefix == md_document_types seeded prefix for every kind (drift guard); ApplicationModules.verify() MUST stay green with no new edges.
decision.ownerActionRequired: none for Q11 itself. (Unrelated adjacent owner gates already tracked elsewhere: ADR-0009 §6 product-owner sign-off on the SPTO/PPTO rename = Q6/OWNER_RELEASE_GATE; and the ADR-0009 §5 doc-fix correcting seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no to the as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no = a documentation correction, not part of this decision. The shared DocumentType enum must reference the AS-BUILT sequence names seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no, since those migrations already ran.)

challenge.reason:
Verified all load-bearing claims against the as-built code/migrations and the actual ADR text; the decision is charter-compliant on all three axes.

AXIS 1 — ADR contradiction: NONE. ADR-0009 §5 + Implementation notes (docs/adr/0009-money-and-numbering.md:109-120) literally specify the exact mechanism the decision builds: `next(DocumentType type)` doing `nextval(type.sequenceName())` with the enum carrying prefix() AND sequenceName(). The decision implements the ADR rather than contradicting it. The ADR-name-vs-as-built trap was checked and the decision is on the CORRECT side: ADR-0009 §5/impl-notes (lines 35, 104-105) say seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no, but the as-built V13 migration (backend/src/main/resources/db/migration/V13__masterdata_document_sequences.sql:33,37) seeded seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no and seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no DO NOT EXIST in the DB. The decision's enum correctly declares SPTO("SPTO","seq_spto_no")/PPTO("PPTO","seq_ppto_no") (as-built names) and explicitly states in ownerActionRequired that the enum must reference the as-built names because those migrations already ran — i.e. it did not trust the stale ADR text. ADR-0008 §1/§6 boundary rules are honoured: shared is verified @ApplicationModule(Type.OPEN) (backend/src/main/java/com/otapp/hmis/shared/package-info.java:7-9), so every module depends on it edge-free; no inventory->billing or pharmacy->billing edge is introduced; no cycle.

AXIS 2 — smuggled deviation: NONE. Every numbering OUTPUT is already ratified and unchanged: the PCN/format/prefix/sequence are byte-identical (current billing impl, backend/.../billing/application/DocumentNumberServiceImpl.java:37-44, already does creditNoteRepository.nextPcnNo() == SELECT nextval('seq_pcn_no'); a generic EntityManager nextval('seq_pcn_no') is functionally identical). The SPTO/PPTO collision fix is pre-ratified (CR-10, seeded V13 + V14 backend/.../V14__seed_p5.sql:44-45, asserted by DocumentTypeIT). The decision actively GUARDS against the classic deviations: it excludes PSO/PCST from the shared service (N17 — they keep verbatim 'PSO/'+id and dual PCST formats), gates PPR/PSR on Q10 resolving server-authoritative, keeps the suffix UNPADDED and the date in EAT, forbids re-creating already-seeded sequences (build starts V39), forbids a second/parallel DocumentNumberService, and mandates a drift-guard IT (enum prefix == md_document_types prefix) which is satisfiable — V14 seeds all 11 kinds with prefixes matching the enum exactly. No hard gate, no FEFO-on-OTC, no destination-batch creation, no new SoD codes are introduced by this decision.

AXIS 3 — classification + owner action: CORRECT. Only the OWNING MODULE/placement of the mechanism changes; no business behaviour, output string, or numbering scheme is touched — that is a pure ARCHITECTURE_DECISION correctly routed to the solution-architect (and flagged as solution-architect-owned in buildImpact, not self-decided). crNeeded:none is correct (no legacy-process deviation). ownerActionRequired:none-for-Q11 is correct, and crucially the decision does NOT silently fold in the two adjacent owner gates — it explicitly leaves the ADR-0009 §6 SPTO/PPTO product-owner release-gate (Q6) and the ADR-0009 §5 doc-fix (seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no -> as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no) as separately-tracked actions, which is the correct handling.
challenge.correction: none — but attach two non-blocking build conditions the implementer must honour (already implied by the decision, recorded here so they are not lost): (1) The shared DocumentType enum MUST use the AS-BUILT sequence names seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no (NOT ADR-0009 §5's stale seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no); the ADR-0009 §5/impl-notes table is a documentation defect to be corrected separately and must never be used as the source for the enum. (2) The drift-guard IT must assert enum.prefix() == md_document_types.prefix for every kind AND that enum.sequenceName() resolves to a sequence that actually exists in the DB (pg sequence existence check), so a future ADR-vs-as-built name drift is caught at test time, not at first nextval. Neither condition changes any output and neither requires a CR or owner sign-off.

ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED (challenge UPHELD). 08a BUILDS unconditionally the legacy worklist FILTER as the exact-process baseline: pharmacy worklist endpoints surface only prescriptions whose linked PatientBill.status is PAID|COVERED for OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER and PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED for INPATIENT (VERIFIED = inpatient credit per D18, NOT insurer-verification), matching legacy PatientResource.java:4347/4364/4381/4410. The clinical dispense terminal (issue_medicine NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN, issued==qty all-or-nothing, hard negative-stock guard, FEFO + PrescriptionBatch lot-trace, stock-card OUT) carries NO terminal bill-status check in the baseline, matching PatientResource.java:3199-3293 exactly. The hard dispense gate is a DEVIATION held OUT of the baseline. IF the engagement-lead elects the hardening it MUST be the already-ratified CR-05 SettlementPolicy.requireSettled(...) read against the prescription LOCAL settled flag (V36, BillSettledEvent-driven) at the issue_medicine seam throwing 422 PAY_BEFORE_SERVICE — NOT via BillingQueries.getBillStatus (forbidden by ADR-0008 §6 Addendum, verified lines 91-93). Reuse existing PAY_BEFORE_SERVICE ErrorCode; do not mint PRESCRIPTION_UNPAID. Flag the as-built PrescriptionRepository comment ('settled filter intentionally omitted, pharmacy queues are different') to the BA as a drift to reconcile with legacy filter behaviour.

RATIFIED WITH CORRECTION (challenge REVISE applied). 08a/08b: pharmacyUid is a per-call parameter that is REQUIRED and server-VALIDATED to resolve (reject missing/unresolvable pharmacy — legacy already does this via NotFoundException at PatientResource.java:3207-3210 and the unconditional deref at PatientServiceImpl.java:3334), used ONLY to select the stock source; endpoint authorization via @PreAuthorize per Q5; pharmacyUid is NOT validated against the authenticated user (NO affiliation check). MUST NOT build any pharmacy_staff / User->Pharmacy / Pharmacist->Pharmacy table, column, V39+ migration, seed, JWT pharmacy claim, or write-time 403 affiliation check; MUST NOT add an I01 'pharmacy_staff seeded' dependency; MUST NOT copy StorePerson->Store onto pharmacy. The user-in-pharmacy affiliation 403 gate is the only DEVIATION and stays behind CR-Q2. RATIFIED (challenge UPHELD). ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED WITH RE-CLASSIFICATION (challenge REVISE applied). The engineering target is correct and retained, but it is NOT auto-decided: README.md line 3 (verified) confirms ALL ADRs including ADR-0017 are status 'Proposed... pending ratification by the engagement lead before they become binding' (ADR-0017 header line 2 = 'Proposed'). The charter no-re-litigate rule fires only for a RATIFIED ADR, so Q4 is NOT ADR_PRE_DECIDED. The legacy stock decrement is a zero-lock check-then-act race (ADR-0017 Context confirms no row lock, no @Version); the pessimistic lock is net-new correctness hardening that preserves the verbatim negative-stock REFUSAL. Re-classified as APPROVED_HARDENING GATED on the engagement-lead's Q4 sign-off (treat as OWNER_RELEASE_GATE until recorded). Build target ONCE ADR-0017 is ratified AND the Q4 deviation is approved: PESSIMISTIC_WRITE row lock on stock-balance + FEFO batches BEFORE the negative-stock guard on every decrement path (clinical dispense, OTC dispense, transfer TO-issue, GRN approve); decrement order lock->re-read->assert qty>=requested (legacy refusal message verbatim, 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK)->decrement balance->FEFO-decrement batches->append stock_card OUT->commit; @Version optimistic default on non-stock transitions with 409 STALE_ENTITY; deterministic balance-before-batches + uid-ordered multi-line locking with lock/statement timeout->409 STOCK_CONTENDED. EXCLUSIONS retained: NULLS-LAST lot selection rides on Q8 (HDE); FEFO-on-OTC stays out of baseline (CR-08); the legacy deductBatch empty-catch swallow is the separate N7 defect-fix, not licensed by Q4.

ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED (challenge UPHELD). GRN VERIFY gating on -ALL/-UPDATE not -APPROVE; pharmacy admin ADMIN-ACCESS + MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE; store ITEM_STOCK-UPDATE; supplier-price SUPPLIER_PRICE_LIST-ALL). ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED (challenge UPHELD). REPRODUCE THE LEGACY GAP in the exact-process baseline. 08b PPRN approve_receipt reproduces PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote (legacy :192-233): increment destination aggregate StockBalance + write ONE inbound StockMovement/stock-card IN row only when receivedQty>0 (N2), and MUST NOT create destination StockBatch rows (the legacy batch loop is commented out :221-232, reproduced absence verbatim). The pharmacy<->STORE PGRN path DOES create destination batches (one per StoreToPharmacyBatch, legacy :230-241) — the asymmetry is intentional and is itself a parity assertion. Golden-master: after p2p RN, destination aggregate balance increased by receivedQty while destination batch-row COUNT is unchanged (batch sum < balance), with NO destination batch and NO error. Clinical risk (inter-pharmacy stock loses lot/expiry traceability at the receiving pharmacy) recorded for HDE; the fix is parked as CR-Q7 (mirror the store path). Audit row still emitted per ADR-0007.

ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED WITH RE-CLASSIFICATION (challenge REVISE applied). Split into three: (1) DATED-LOT FEFO ORDER + NULL-EXPIRY HANDLING (clinical issue_medicine + p2p/p2s transfer-issue): the TARGET is lockFefoForDispense ordering (expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST, id ASC) under PESSIMISTIC_WRITE with negative-stock guard after the lock, per ADR-0017 §2. BUT this is NOT flatly ADR_PRE_DECIDED: ADR-0017 is a concurrency/locking ADR whose 'Exact-process impact' section explicitly disclaims any business-outcome change ('None on business outcomes... a correctness improvement, not a behaviour change'), yet NULLS-LAST is verifiably NOT behaviour-neutral — it changes WHICH lot dispenses (legacy getEarlierBatch PatientResource.java:3338-3376 silently EXCLUDES null-expiry lots whenever a dated lot exists; NULLS-LAST now consumes a previously-stranded lot after dated stock). Selecting a no-recorded-expiry lot for dispense is an HDE clinical-safety call (Q8 owner = HDE, no inc-08 DECISIONS-RATIFIED closes it). RECLASSIFIED as PARTIALLY ADR-decided (mechanism + dated-lot order ratified by ADR-0017 §2 once ADR-0017 is ratified) + DEVIATION_NEEDS_OWNER_SIGN-OFF for the NULLS-LAST clinical behaviour change (explicit HDE sign-off + golden-master parity exception; disclosure-register entry necessary but NOT sufficient). Until HDE signs off, the 08a baseline reproduces the VERIFIED legacy null-expiry EXCLUSION (dated lots only; null-expiry lots stranded). (2) id-ASC tiebreak: ADR-silent determinism pin, no separate CR. (3) FEFO-ON-OTC: do NOT build (legacy give_medicine deductBatch commented out :6317) — DEVIATION behind CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC; OTC stays aggregate-decrement + stock-card OUT only, no batch consumption, no lot-trace. Lot-trace (PrescriptionBatch-equivalent) rows must NOT be silently dropped on the clinical path.
ratifiedDecisions.classification: PARTIALLY ADR_PRE_DECIDED (mechanism + dated-lot order, ADR-0017 §2) + DEVIATION_NEEDS_OWNER_SIGN-OFF (NULLS-LAST, HDE) + DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR (FEFO-on-OTC, CR-08)

ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED WITH CORRECTION (challenge REVISE applied). PRICING/PAYMENT (sub-question B, UPHELD unchanged): OTC bill amount stays flat Money(Medicine.price).multiply(qty) at NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP (ADR-0009 §2), NO insurance/co-pay/discount/waiver; route via billing::api.recordClinicalCharge with ChargeRequest.paymentType = literal CASH and the incoming payment-mode value DELIBERATELY IGNORED (legacy PatientServiceImpl.java:3342); bills on the GENERAL dummy patient; consequently the ADR-0010 collection-by-payment-method OTC slice renders all-CASH in the baseline and reporting must not invent multi-mode OTC breakdowns; the real-payment-mode improvement is parked as a CR. NUMBERING CORRECTION (sub-question A): reproduce the verbatim OUTPUT FORMAT STRINGS PSO/{n}, PCST{n} (savePharmacySaleOrder path) and PCST/{year}/{n} (createPharmacyCustomer path) — forward slash, no date, no Formater, no zero-pad, KEEP both divergent customer formats; these stay OUTSIDE the shared DocumentNumberService (ADR-0009 silent on OTC, N17). REMOVE the original instruction that 'the {n} suffix = the row's own PK' — exposing the surrogate id violates ADR-0014 §1 (verified line 35, MANDATORY hidden-id: id MUST NOT appear in any DTO/response/URL/client-facing value) and is internally inconsistent with 'no double-save' (the legacy PK suffix requires the save-then-renumber double-save). Treat the numeric-suffix PROVENANCE as the same CR-02-style decoupling already ratified for MRNO: substitute a dedicated PostgreSQL sequence (new seq_pso_no/seq_pcst_no via a V39+ migration — verified NOT present in V13; one atomic nextval, single-insert, no double-save). Because PSO/5 will no longer imply PK 5 this is an observable change = a flagged numbering-provenance CR (MRNO/CR-02 precedent), NOT a silent baseline item. QA asserts the verbatim format strings and dual PCST shapes, all-CASH OTC, NOT a PK-equals-suffix value.

CONTRACT-PRESERVING CONCURRENCY DEFENSE that IS in scope NOW: (a) back the PREVIEW endpoint with seq_ppr_no/seq_psr_no (ADR-0009 §5, verified V13:49,53) and the verbatim PREFIX{yyyyMMdd EAT}-{seq} format, NOT MAX(id)+1; (b) ADD a DB UNIQUE constraint on the RO no column (legacy already carries @Column(unique=true)) surfacing a hard 409/422 on collision. ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED (challenge UPHELD). PROMOTE DocumentNumberService + DocumentType to the SHARED KERNEL (com.otapp.hmis.shared.documentnumber) and DECOUPLE the sequence read from any module's domain repository. The impl issues a generic EntityManager nextval(:seqName) keyed off an allow-list of sequence names DECLARED on the DocumentType enum (enum is the only source; native string never built from caller input), formats prefix+yyyyMMdd(EAT)+'-'+seq unpadded with a test-overridable Clock, keeping billing's seq_pcn_no behaviour byte-identical. Delete billing.application's DocumentType + DocumentNumberService(+Impl) and rewire CreditNoteService to the shared bean. shared is @ApplicationModule(Type.OPEN) (verified) so pharmacy/inventory/billing depend on it edge-free — NO inventory->billing or pharmacy->billing edge for numbering; ApplicationModules.verify() stays green. The md_document_types prefix REGISTRY stays in masterdata (reference/config data); the numbering MECHANISM (allocation + format) is the cross-cutting concern that belongs in shared. Pure ownership/placement change — no business behaviour, output string, or numbering scheme touched (all ratified by ADR-0009 §5/§6, CR-09, CR-10). BUILD CONDITIONS: (1) the shared DocumentType enum MUST use the AS-BUILT sequence names seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no (NOT ADR-0009 §5's stale seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no); (2) a drift-guard IT must assert enum.prefix() == md_document_types prefix for every kind AND that enum.sequenceName() resolves to a sequence that exists in the DB. PSO/PCST stay OUTSIDE the shared service (N17); PPR/PSR join only if Q10 resolves server-authoritative. Build starts at V39 — do NOT re-create the V13-seeded sequences.

changeRequests: CR-Q2-PHARMACY-STAFF-AFFILIATION: Introduce net-new pharmacist->pharmacy (pharmacy_staff M:N) affiliation enforced as a write-time 403 scoping gate — DEVIATION from legacy client-trust on pharmacyUid. Owner approves scope, rules whether pharmacy may be stricter than stores (which use only a soft StorePerson->Store filter, never a write gate), assigns the increment owning the schema/seed, gates the affiliation-write endpoint with ADMIN-ACCESS, and updates I01 traceability. NOTE: ADR-0020 §Decision.4 currently EXCLUDES pharmacist affiliation, so this CR also requires amending/superseding ADR-0020.

CR-03-THREE-WAY-MATCH (NEW FEATURE): Net-new three-way procurement match — SupplierInvoice entity + GRN-detail invoice-qty capture + payment-release gate + /three-way-match/validate endpoint + SupplierInvoice DocumentType/sequence. Safety-critical financial; zero legacy basis (PHANTOM D11). Requires engagement-lead + finance/HDE approval; finance/HDE must supply the reconciliation semantics (invoice-qty source, tolerance rules, what 'payment release' gates and who authorizes) since there is no legacy oracle.

CR-08-Q4-STOCK-LOCKING: Net-new PESSIMISTIC_WRITE row lock + @Version split on stock decrement (legacy is zero-lock check-then-act). Correctness hardening that preserves the verbatim negative-stock refusal but is a deviation from observed legacy behaviour; requires written approval before 08a/08b implement the lock. Mirrors the inc-04 CR-05 treatment of a net-new gate where legacy only had a softer control.

CR-08-SoD: Introduce per-transition segregation-of-duties privilege codes for LPO/GRN/transfer state changes (net-new codes beyond the ratified 35; no legacy basis). Raise ONLY if the owner wants SoD; otherwise confirm coarse-gating-verbatim is frozen. (The deny-by-default closure does NOT need a CR — ADR-0006-ratified hardening.)

CR-Q7-P2P-DESTINATION-BATCHES: Create destination StockBatch (lot/expiry) rows on pharmacy<->pharmacy receiving-note approval to restore inter-pharmacy expiry/lot traceability (mirror the pharmacy<->store RN path). Clinical-safety DEVIATION from legacy (legacy batch loop commented out); requires HDE + engagement-owner approval before any 08b code.

CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC: Apply unified FEFO batch consumption + lot-traceability to walk-in OTC PharmacySaleOrder dispense (legacy deductBatch commented out — net-new). DEVIATION; do NOT build in the 08a baseline until approved.

CR-09-NUM1-OTC-NUMBERING-PROVENANCE (ADR-0014-forced): Decouple OTC document-number numeric suffix from the surrogate PK — substitute dedicated PostgreSQL sequences seq_pso_no/seq_pcst_no (new V39+ migration; one atomic nextval, single-insert) for the legacy PK suffix, because ADR-0014 §1 forbids exposing id in any client-facing value. Observable change (PSO/5 no longer implies PK 5); same engagement-lead-approved decoupling already ratified for MRNO as CR-02. MAY be packaged with an optional unification of the dual PCST formats (PCST{n} vs PCST/{year}/{n}).

CR-09-NUM2-OTC-PAYMENT-MODE: Honor real OTC payment mode at billing (stop hardcoding ChargeRequest.paymentType=CASH; persist the incoming payment mode) to enable a true OTC-revenue-by-payment-mode report — supersedes/implements BILL-5. Owner must note this is the prerequisite that turns the ADR-0010 collection-by-payment-method OTC slice from all-CASH into a true by-mode report.

CR-Q10-PPR-PSR-SERVER-AUTHORITATIVE: Make PPR/PSR request-order numbers server-authoritative (drop client-supplied request_no; assign via DocumentNumberService.next() inside the insert tx) — aligns all 8 doc types and removes the client-trust surface. Behaviour change to the RO request contract, OpenAPI body, and Angular preview flow. Owner = engagement-lead.
ownerActionsRequired: RATIFY THE ADRs (root release gate): README.md line 3 confirms ALL ADRs are status 'Proposed... pending ratification by the engagement lead before they become binding.' The charter no-re-litigate rule fires only for RATIFIED ADRs. The engagement-lead must formally ratify at minimum ADR-0017 (concurrency/locking, blocks Q4 + the Q8 mechanism), ADR-0006, ADR-0008, ADR-0009, ADR-0014, ADR-0020 — and reconcile the file 'Status' headers (all still read 'Proposed', incl. ADR-0017 line 2) with whatever is treated as binding. Until done, Q4 and Q8's ADR-backed targets are parked, not baseline.

Q1 (CR-05 extension to pharmacy dispense): decide whether CR-05's pay-before-service hard gate EXTENDS to the pharmacy dispense terminal (deviation from legacy filter-only), with HDE confirming no emergency/inpatient/insured dispensing regression. Until sign-off, 08a ships filter-only. HDE position: the SettlementPolicy local-flag gate WITH its insurance/inpatient/emergency carve-outs is clinically safe and recommended; a getBillStatus-based or blanket gate is NOT permitted (ADR-0008 §6 Addendum).

Q2: decide whether to raise/approve CR-Q2 (pharmacy_staff affiliation 403 gate); rule on stricter-than-stores vs parity; assign the increment owning the schema/seed; update I01 traceability. Separately, because ADR-0020 (Proposed) already mandates a server-required working-pharmacy pharmacyUid AND excludes pharmacist affiliation, either ratify ADR-0020 as-is (build presence-required/validated pharmacyUid, no affiliation) or approve CR-Q2/amend ADR-0020 for a stricter gate.

Q3: engagement-lead + finance/HDE decide whether the net-new three-way match feature is wanted at all; if yes, approve CR-03 at the phase gate BEFORE any code and finance/HDE supply the reconciliation semantics. No owner action is required to proceed with the baseline drop.
Q4: approve the Q4 deviation (pessimistic lock + @Version-on-transitions split) AND ratify ADR-0017 before 08a/08b implement the lock. Until then the lock is parked, not baseline.

Q5: decide whether per-transition SoD is wanted (if yes approve CR-08-SoD before any code; if no, confirm coarse-gating-verbatim is frozen). Acknowledge that closing the default-open posture, while ADR-0006-ratified, produces an observable behaviour delta (formerly anonymous/ungated flows now require auth) that the parity harness labels net-new hardening, not reproduction.

Q6 (ADR-0009 §6 release gate): give explicit product-owner sign-off on the SPTO and PPTO prefixes (the V13/V14 migration side is already complete) and confirm the external/printed-document/audit-export 'SPT' sweep is identified and updated to SPTO/PPTO before 08b release.

Q7: engagement-owner + HDE decide whether to approve CR-Q7 (p2p destination-batch creation). Until approved, 08b ships the reproduced gap. HDE recommends raising it (real medicines-management/patient-safety weakness — no destination expiry tracking or lot recall for inter-pharmacy stock).

Q8 (HDE clinical sign-off): the HDE must explicitly approve the NULLS-LAST behaviour change (dispensing a previously-stranded null-expiry lot after dated stock), recorded as a deliberate, approved behaviour change with a golden-master parity exception. Until HDE signs off, 08a reproduces the verified legacy null-expiry EXCLUSION. Also approve CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC separately if FEFO-on-OTC is wanted.

Q9: approve (or reject) the two/three OTC CRs at the phase gate before any normalization or real-payment-mode behaviour is implemented. NOTE: even merely reproducing the OTC number format LEGALLY requires the ADR-0014-forced suffix-provenance decoupling (CR-09-NUM1) because the legacy PK suffix cannot be exposed — so this CR must be flagged even though the visible format strings are reproduced verbatim. Until then 08a builds the verbatim-legacy format strings backed by dedicated sequences.

Q10: decide CR-Q10 (server-authoritative PPR/PSR numbering). Until approval, the exact-process baseline (client-supplied preview-then-supply + UNIQUE-constraint hardening) stands and 08b builds to it.
register.baselineScopeEffect:
## IN the 08a/08b exact-process baseline (build now, verbatim)

**08a (dispensing + stock core):**
- Pharmacy worklist as a FILTER (PAID|COVERED for OUT/OUTSIDER; +VERIFIED for INPATIENT). Dispense terminal with NO terminal bill-status check (Q1).
- pharmacyUid as a REQUIRED, server-VALIDATED per-call parameter used ONLY to select the stock source; reject missing/unresolvable pharmacy; NO user-affiliation check (Q2).
- Coarse RBAC gating reusing ONLY the seeded 35 privilege codes, reproduced verbatim per endpoint/method; deny-by-default (authenticated() minimum) closure (Q5).
- Clinical dispense: NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN, issued==qty all-or-nothing, hard negative-stock guard, PrescriptionBatch lot-trace, stock-card OUT (Q1/Q8).
- FEFO dated-lot order under the lock per ADR-0017 §2 ONCE ratified+approved; **null-expiry handling = the VERIFIED legacy EXCLUSION (dated lots only) until HDE signs off NULLS-LAST** (Q8). id-ASC tiebreak pinned.
- OTC dispense: aggregate decrement + stock-card OUT only, NO batch consumption, NO lot-trace; flat price×qty at NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP; paymentType=CASH literal, incoming mode ignored; GENERAL dummy patient; ADR-0010 OTC slice all-CASH (Q8/Q9).
- OTC numbering: verbatim format strings PSO/{n}, PCST{n}, PCST/{year}/{n} — but suffix backed by dedicated sequences (CR-flagged provenance), NOT the raw PK (Q9, ADR-0014 §1).

**08b (store/procurement/transfers):**
- GRN per-line NOT-VERIFIED->VERIFIED gated by [receivedQty<=orderedQty] AND [sum(GRNDetailBatch.qty)==detail.receivedQty]; header PENDING->APPROVED only; approve() hard-gates every line VERIFIED; one-GRN-per-LPO + store-match + LPO==SUBMITTED (Q3 — the REAL gate).
- SPTO/PPTO emission (never 'SPT'); bind to as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no via DocumentNumberServiceImpl switch + new enum constants (Q6).
- p2p PPRN approve: aggregate StockBalance increment + ONE IN movement (when receivedQty>0), NO destination StockBatch rows; pharmacy<->store PGRN DOES create destination batches (intentional asymmetry, Q7).
- PPR/PSR: client-supplied preview-then-supply contract preserved; preview backed by seq_ppr_no/seq_psr_no; DB UNIQUE constraint on RO no -> 409/422 (Q10).
- Same lock+guard discipline on transfer TO-issue + GRN-approve increments ONCE Q4 approved (Q4/Q8).

**Shared/architecture prerequisite (before 08a/08b):** promote DocumentNumberService + DocumentType to shared kernel, decoupled from domain repos, no new module edges (Q11).

## PARKED as CR (NOT in the baseline)
- Hard dispense pay-gate (Q1 — only via CR-05/SettlementPolicy local flag, owner-gated).
- pharmacy_staff affiliation 403 gate (CR-Q2).
- Three-way match / SupplierInvoice / payment-release (CR-03).
- Pessimistic lock + @Version split (CR-08-Q4 — engineering retained but gated on approval + ADR-0017 ratification).
- Per-transition SoD codes (CR-08-SoD).
- p2p destination-batch creation (CR-Q7).
- FEFO-on-OTC (CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC).
- NULLS-LAST lot selection (Q8 — gated on HDE sign-off; baseline reproduces legacy exclusion until then).
- OTC numbering normalization to shared DocumentNumberService + dual-PCST unification + real OTC payment mode (CR-09-NUM1/NUM2).
- Server-authoritative PPR/PSR numbering (CR-Q10).

ADR-0009 Implementation-notes SQL block lines 104-105: rename the two CREATE SEQUENCE statements to seq_spto_no (-- StoreToPharmacy TO -> SPTO) and seq_ppto_no (-- PharmacyToPharmacy TO -> PPTO).
